gold_answer: Verse 1.5 teaches that hatred does not cease by hatred at any time; it ceases by love, an old rule.
question: Which verse states that hatred never ceases by hatred but only ceases by love, calling this an old rule?

hi.gold_answer: श्लोक 1.5 सिखाता है कि घृणा किसी भी समय घृणा से समाप्त नहीं होती है; यह प्रेम से समाप्त होती है, जो एक पुराना नियम है।
hi.question: कौन सा श्लोक कहता है कि घृणा कभी भी घृणा से समाप्त नहीं होती है, बल्कि केवल प्रेम से समाप्त होती है, इसे एक पुराना नियम कहते हैं?

pi.gold_answer: Gāthā 1.5 deseti yaṁ veraṁ verena kismiñci kāle na sammati; averena sammati, esa dhammo sanantano.
pi.question: Katamā gāthā vadati yaṁ veraṁ verena na kudācanaṁ sammati, averena pana sammati, etaṁ sanantanaṁ dhammaṁ nāma?

_gold_src.text_en: For hatred does not cease by hatred at any time: hatred ceases by love, this is an old rule.
_gold_src.original: Na hi verena verāni, sammantīdha kudācanaṁ; Averena ca sammanti, esa dhammo sanantano.

_nearmiss_src.text_en: "He abused me, he beat me, he defeated me, he robbed me,"—in those who harbour such thoughts hatred will never cease.
_nearmiss_src.original: Akkocchi maṁ avadhi maṁ, ajini maṁ ahāsi me; Ye ca taṁ upanayhanti, veraṁ tesaṁ na sammati.

_nearmiss_src.text_en: "He abused me, he beat me, he defeated me, he robbed me,"—in those who do not harbour such thoughts hatred will cease.
_nearmiss_src.original: Akkocchi maṁ avadhi maṁ, ajini maṁ ahāsi me; Ye ca taṁ nupanayhanti, veraṁ tesūpasammati.

gold_answer: Verse 1.1 says we are made of our thoughts, and that pain follows an evil thought like the wheel following the ox's foot.
question: Which verse opens by saying that all we are is the result of our thoughts, and warns that if one acts with an evil thought, pain follows as the wheel follows the ox that draws the carriage?

hi.gold_answer: श्लोक 1 कहता है कि हम अपने विचारों से बने हैं, और यह दर्द एक बुरे विचार का अनुसरण करता है जैसे बैल के पैर के पीछे चलने वाला पहिया।
hi.question: कौन सा श्लोक यह कहने से शुरू होता है कि हम सभी हमारे विचारों का परिणाम हैं, और चेतावनी देता है कि अगर कोई बुरे विचार के साथ काम करता है, तो दर्द होता है क्योंकि पहिया उस बैल का पीछा करता है जो गाड़ी खींचता है?

pi.gold_answer: Gāthā 1.1 vadati yaṁ mayaṁ manomayā homa, paduṭṭhañca manaṁ dukkhaṁ anveti cakkaṁ viya vahato padaṁ.
pi.question: Katamā gāthā ādimhi vadati yaṁ sabbe dhammā manopubbaṅgamā manomayā, yo ca paduṭṭhena manasā karoti, taṁ dukkhaṁ anveti cakkaṁ viya vahato padaṁ?

_gold_src.text_en: All that we are is the result of what we have thought: it is founded on our thoughts, it is made up of our thoughts. If a man speaks or acts with an evil thought, pain follows him, as the wheel follows the foot of the ox that draws the carriage.
_gold_src.original: Manopubbaṅgamā dhammā, manoseṭṭhā manomayā; Manasā ce paduṭṭhena, bhāsati vā karoti vā; Tato naṁ dukkhamanveti, cakkaṁva vahato padaṁ.

_nearmiss_src.text_en: All that we are is the result of what we have thought: it is founded on our thoughts, it is made up of our thoughts. If a man speaks or acts with a pure thought, happiness follows him, like a shadow that never leaves him.
_nearmiss_src.original: Manopubbaṅgamā dhammā, manoseṭṭhā manomayā; Manasā ce pasannena, bhāsati vā karoti vā; Tato naṁ sukhamanveti, chāyāva anapāyinī.

_nearmiss_src.text_en: As rain breaks through an ill-thatched house, passion will break through an unreflecting mind.
_nearmiss_src.original: Yathā agāraṁ ducchannaṁ, vuṭṭhī samativijjhati; Evaṁ abhāvitaṁ cittaṁ, rāgo samativijjhati.

gold_answer: Verse 1.2 states that happiness follows a pure thought like a shadow that never departs.
question: Which verse says that if one acts with a pure thought, happiness follows like a shadow that never leaves?

hi.gold_answer: श्लोक 1.2 में कहा गया है कि खुशी एक छाया की तरह एक शुद्ध विचार का अनुसरण करती है जो कभी नहीं जाती है।
hi.question: कौन सा श्लोक कहता है कि यदि कोई शुद्ध विचार के साथ कार्य करता है, तो खुशी एक छाया की तरह आती है जो कभी नहीं छोड़ती है?

pi.gold_answer: Gāthā 1.2 vadati yaṁ sukhaṁ pasannaṁ manaṁ anveti chāyā viya anapāyinī.
pi.question: Katamā gāthā vadati yaṁ sace pasannena manasā karoti, sukhaṁ taṁ anveti chāyā viya anapāyinī?

_gold_src.text_en: All that we are is the result of what we have thought: it is founded on our thoughts, it is made up of our thoughts. If a man speaks or acts with a pure thought, happiness follows him, like a shadow that never leaves him.
_gold_src.original: Manopubbaṅgamā dhammā, manoseṭṭhā manomayā; Manasā ce pasannena, bhāsati vā karoti vā; Tato naṁ sukhamanveti, chāyāva anapāyinī.

_nearmiss_src.text_en: All that we are is the result of what we have thought: it is founded on our thoughts, it is made up of our thoughts. If a man speaks or acts with an evil thought, pain follows him, as the wheel follows the foot of the ox that draws the carriage.
_nearmiss_src.original: Manopubbaṅgamā dhammā, manoseṭṭhā manomayā; Manasā ce paduṭṭhena, bhāsati vā karoti vā; Tato naṁ dukkhamanveti, cakkaṁva vahato padaṁ.

_nearmiss_src.text_en: The virtuous man delights in this world, and he delights in the next; he delights in both. He delights and rejoices, when he sees the purity of his own work.
_nearmiss_src.original: Idha modati pecca modati, Katapuñño ubhayattha modati; So modati so pamodati, Disvā kammavisuddhimattano.

gold_answer: Verse 2.1 calls earnestness the path of Nirvana (immortality) and thoughtlessness the path of death.
question: Which verse declares earnestness to be the path of immortality and thoughtlessness the path of death, saying the earnest do not die?

hi.gold_answer: श्लोक 2 गम्भीरता को निर्वाण (अमरता) और विचारहीनता को मृत्यु का मार्ग कहता है।
hi.question: कौन सा श्लोक ईमानदारी को अमरता और विचारहीनता का मार्ग मृत्यु का मार्ग घोषित करता है, जिसमें कहा गया है कि गंभीर लोग नहीं मरते हैं?

pi.gold_answer: Gāthā 2.1 appamādaṁ amatapadaṁ (nibbānassa maggaṁ) vadati, pamādañca maccuno padaṁ.
pi.question: Katamā gāthā vadati yaṁ appamādo amatapadaṁ, pamādo maccuno padaṁ, appamattā ca na mīyanti?

_gold_src.text_en: Earnestness is the path of immortality (Nirvana), thoughtlessness the path of death. Those who are in earnest do not die, those who are thoughtless are as if dead already.
_gold_src.original: Appamādo amatapadaṁ, pamādo maccuno padaṁ; Appamattā na mīyanti, ye pamattā yathā matā.

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Fools follow after vanity, men of evil wisdom. The wise man keeps earnestness as his best jewel.
_nearmiss_src.original: Pamādamanuyuñjanti, bālā dummedhino janā; Appamādañca medhāvī, dhanaṁ seṭṭhaṁva rakkhati.

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Earnest among the thoughtless, awake among the sleepers, the wise man advances like a racer, leaving behind the hack.
_nearmiss_src.original: Appamatto pamattesu, suttesu bahujāgaro; Abalassaṁva sīghasso, hitvā yāti sumedhaso.

gold_answer: Verse 3.1 likens the wise man straightening his trembling thought to a fletcher straightening an arrow.
question: Which verse compares the wise man straightening his unsteady thought to a fletcher making his arrow straight?

hi.gold_answer: श्लोक 3.1 में बुद्धिमान व्यक्ति द्वारा अपने कांपते हुए विचार को सीधा करने की तुलना तीर को सीधा करने वाले एक झंडे से की गई है।
hi.question: किस श्लोक में बुद्धिमान व्यक्ति द्वारा अपने अस्थिर विचार को सीधा करने की तुलना तीर चलाने वाले से की गई है?

pi.gold_answer: Gāthā 3.1 medhāviṁ phandanaṁ cittaṁ ujuṁ karontaṁ usukāraṁ viya, yo tejanaṁ (saraṁ) ujuṁ karoti, upameti.
pi.question: Katamā gāthā medhāviṁ phandanaṁ capalaṁ cittaṁ ujuṁ karontaṁ usukāraṁ viya saraṁ ujuṁ karontaṁ upameti?

_gold_src.text_en: As a fletcher makes straight his arrow, a wise man makes straight his trembling and unsteady thought, which is difficult to guard, difficult to hold back.
_gold_src.original: Phandanaṁ capalaṁ cittaṁ, dūrakkhaṁ dunnivārayaṁ; Ujuṁ karoti medhāvī, usukārova tejanaṁ.

_nearmiss_src.text_en: It is good to tame the mind, which is difficult to hold in and flighty, rushing wherever it listeth; a tamed mind brings happiness.
_nearmiss_src.original: Dunniggahassa lahuno, yatthakāmanipātino; Cittassa damatho sādhu, cittaṁ dantaṁ sukhāvahaṁ.

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Let the wise man guard his thoughts, for they are difficult to perceive, very artful, and they rush wherever they list: thoughts well guarded bring happiness.
_nearmiss_src.original: Sududdasaṁ sunipuṇaṁ, yatthakāmanipātinaṁ; Cittaṁ rakkhetha medhāvī, cittaṁ guttaṁ sukhāvahaṁ.

gold_answer: Verse 4.6 says a sage should live in his village like a bee that takes nectar without injuring the flower.
question: Which verse advises that a sage should dwell in his village as the bee gathers nectar without harming the flower's colour or scent?

hi.gold_answer: श्लोक 4.6 में कहा गया है कि एक ऋषि को अपने गाँव में एक मधुमक्खी की तरह रहना चाहिए जो फूल को नुकसान पहुँचाए बिना अमृत लेती है।
hi.question: कौन सा श्लोक सलाह देता है कि एक ऋषि को अपने गाँव में रहना चाहिए क्योंकि मधुमक्खी फूल के रंग या सुगंध को नुकसान पहुँचाए बिना अमृत इकट्ठा करती है?

pi.gold_answer: Gāthā 4.6 vadati yaṁ munī gāme care bhamaro viya yo pupphaṁ aheṭhayaṁ rasaṁ ādāya gacchati.
pi.question: Katamā gāthā vadati yaṁ munī gāme tathā care yathā bhamaro pupphassa vaṇṇagandhaṁ aheṭhayaṁ rasaṁ ādāya paleti?

_gold_src.text_en: As the bee collects nectar and departs without injuring the flower, or its colour or scent, so let a sage dwell in his village.
_gold_src.original: Yathāpi bhamaro pupphaṁ, vaṇṇagandhamaheṭhayaṁ; Paleti rasamādāya, evaṁ gāme munī care.

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Death carries off a man who is gathering flowers and whose mind is distracted, as a flood carries off a sleeping village.
_nearmiss_src.original: Pupphāni heva pacinantaṁ, byāsattamanasaṁ naraṁ; Suttaṁ gāmaṁ mahoghova, maccu ādāya gacchati.

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Like a beautiful flower, full of colour, but without scent, are the fine but fruitless words of him who does not act accordingly.
_nearmiss_src.original: Yathāpi ruciraṁ pupphaṁ, vaṇṇavantaṁ agandhakaṁ; Evaṁ subhāsitā vācā, aphalā hoti akubbato.

gold_answer: Verse 4.11 contrasts flower-scent, which cannot go against the wind, with the odour of the good, which travels everywhere.
question: Which verse observes that the scent of flowers cannot travel against the wind, but the fragrance of good people spreads even against the wind?

hi.gold_answer: श्लोक 4.11 फूल-गंध की तुलना करता है, जो हवा के खिलाफ नहीं जा सकता है, अच्छे की गंध के साथ, जो हर जगह घूमती है।
hi.question: कौन सा श्लोक बताता है कि फूलों की सुगंध हवा के खिलाफ नहीं चल सकती है, लेकिन अच्छे लोगों की सुगंध हवा के खिलाफ भी फैलती है?

pi.gold_answer: Gāthā 4.11 pupphagandhaṁ, yo paṭivātaṁ na eti, satañca gandhena, yo sabbā disā pavāyati, viseseti.
pi.question: Katamā gāthā vadati yaṁ pupphagandho paṭivātaṁ na eti, satañca gandho paṭivātampi eti?

_gold_src.text_en: The scent of flowers does not travel against the wind, nor (that of) sandal-wood, or of Tagara and Mallika flowers; but the odour of good people travels even against the wind; a good man pervades every place.
_gold_src.original: Na pupphagandho paṭivātameti, Na candanaṁ tagaramallikā vā; Satañca gandho paṭivātameti, Sabbā disā sappuriso pavāyati.

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Sandal-wood or Tagara, a lotus-flower, or a Vassiki, among these sorts of perfumes, the perfume of virtue is unsurpassed.
_nearmiss_src.original: Candanaṁ tagaraṁ vāpi, uppalaṁ atha vassikī; Etesaṁ gandhajātānaṁ, sīlagandho anuttaro.

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Mean is the scent that comes from Tagara and sandal-wood;—the perfume of those who possess virtue rises up to the gods as the highest.
_nearmiss_src.original: Appamatto ayaṁ gandho, yāyaṁ tagaracandanī; Yo ca sīlavataṁ gandho, vāti devesu uttamo.

gold_answer: Verse 8.4 declares the conqueror of self the greatest of conquerors, above one who defeats a million men.
question: Which verse says that conquering oneself is a greater victory than conquering a thousand times a thousand men in battle?

hi.gold_answer: श्लोक 8.4 स्वयं के विजेता को विजेताओं में सबसे बड़ा घोषित करता है, उससे ऊपर जो दस लाख पुरुषों को हराता है।
hi.question: कौन सा श्लोक कहता है कि खुद को जीतना युद्ध में एक हजार बार एक हजार पुरुषों को जीतने से बड़ी जीत है?

pi.gold_answer: Gāthā 8.4 attajinaṁ saṅgāmajuttamaṁ vadati, yo sahassaṁ mānusānaṁ jinantaṁ atikkamati.
pi.question: Katamā gāthā vadati yaṁ attano jayo seyyo yaṁ saṅgāme sahassaṁ sahassena mānusānaṁ jayā?

_gold_src.text_en: If one man conquer in battle a thousand times thousand men, and if another conquer himself, he is the greatest of conquerors.
_gold_src.original: Yo sahassaṁ sahassena, saṅgāme mānuse jine; Ekañca jeyyamattānaṁ, sa ve saṅgāmajuttamo.

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Even though a speech be a thousand (of words), but made up of senseless words, one word of sense is better, which if a man hears, he becomes quiet.
_nearmiss_src.original: Sahassamapi ce vācā, anatthapadasaṁhitā; Ekaṁ atthapadaṁ seyyo, yaṁ sutvā upasammati.

_nearmiss_src.text_en: If a man for a hundred years sacrifice month after month with a thousand, and if he but for one moment pay homage to a man whose soul is grounded (in true knowledge), better is that homage than sacrifice for a hundred years.
_nearmiss_src.original: Māse māse sahassena, yo yajetha sataṁ samaṁ; Ekañca bhāvitattānaṁ, muhuttamapi pūjaye; Sāyeva pūjanā seyyo, yañce vassasataṁ hutaṁ.

gold_answer: Verse 9.6 cautions that evil accumulates like falling water-drops filling a pot.
question: Which verse warns against thinking lightly of evil, since even a water-pot is filled drop by drop and a fool fills himself with evil little by little?

hi.gold_answer: आयत 9.6 में आगाह किया गया है कि बुराई एक बर्तन में पानी की बूंदें गिरने की तरह जमा हो जाती है।
hi.question: कौन सा श्लोक बुराई के बारे में हल्के में सोचने के खिलाफ चेतावनी देता है, क्योंकि एक पानी का बर्तन भी बूंद-बूंद भर जाता है और एक मूर्ख धीरे-धीरे खुद को बुराई से भर देता है?

pi.gold_answer: Gāthā 9.6 vadati yaṁ pāpaṁ udabindūnaṁ nipātena kumbhaṁ pūrentaṁ viya thokaṁ thokaṁ upacīyati.
pi.question: Katamā gāthā pāpaṁ na avamaññituṁ vadati, yasmā udabindunipātena udakumbho pūrati, bālo ca thokaṁ thokaṁ pāpassa pūrati?

_gold_src.text_en: Let no man think lightly of evil, saying in his heart, It will not come nigh unto me. Even by the falling of water-drops a water-pot is filled; the fool becomes full of evil, even if he gather it little by little.
_gold_src.original: Māvamaññetha pāpassa, na mantaṁ āgamissati; Udabindunipātena, udakumbhopi pūrati; Bālo pūrati pāpassa, thokaṁ thokampi ācinaṁ.

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Let no man think lightly of good, saying in his heart, It will not come nigh unto me. Even by the falling of water-drops a water-pot is filled; the wise man becomes full of good, even if he gather it little by little.
_nearmiss_src.original: Māvamaññetha puññassa, na mandaṁ āgamissati; Udabindunipātena, udakumbhopi pūrati; Dhīro pūrati puññassa, thokaṁ thokampi ācinaṁ.

_nearmiss_src.text_en: If a man commits a sin, let him not do it again; let him not delight in sin: pain is the outcome of evil.
_nearmiss_src.original: Pāpañce puriso kayirā, Na naṁ kayirā punappunaṁ; Na tamhi chandaṁ kayirātha, Dukkho pāpassa uccayo.

gold_answer: Verse 10.1 reasons that since all fear death, one should not kill nor cause killing.
question: Which verse says that all beings tremble at punishment and fear death, urging that one should therefore not kill or cause slaughter?

hi.gold_answer: पद 10.1 का कारण यह है कि चूंकि सभी को मृत्यु का डर है, इसलिए किसी को न तो मारना चाहिए और न ही हत्या का कारण बनना चाहिए।
hi.question: कौन सा श्लोक कहता है कि सभी प्राणी सजा से कांपते हैं और मृत्यु से डरते हैं, और आग्रह करते हैं कि इसलिए किसी को मारना या वध नहीं करना चाहिए?

pi.gold_answer: Gāthā 10.1 vadati yaṁ yasmā sabbe maccuno bhāyanti, tasmā attānaṁ upamaṁ katvā na haneyya na ghātaye.
pi.question: Katamā gāthā vadati yaṁ sabbe daṇḍassa tasanti maccuno ca bhāyanti, tasmā na haneyya na ghātaye?

_gold_src.text_en: All men tremble at punishment, all men fear death; remember that you are like unto them, and do not kill, nor cause slaughter.
_gold_src.original: Sabbe tasanti daṇḍassa, sabbe bhāyanti maccuno; Attānaṁ upamaṁ katvā, na haneyya na ghātaye.

_nearmiss_src.text_en: All men tremble at punishment, all men love life; remember that thou art like unto them, and do not kill, nor cause slaughter.
_nearmiss_src.original: Sabbe tasanti daṇḍassa, sabbesaṁ jīvitaṁ piyaṁ; Attānaṁ upamaṁ katvā, na haneyya na ghātaye.

_nearmiss_src.text_en: As a cowherd with his staff drives his cows into the stable, so do Age and Death drive the life of men.
_nearmiss_src.original: Yathā daṇḍena gopālo, gāvo pājeti gocaraṁ; Evaṁ jarā ca maccu ca, āyuṁ pājenti pāṇinaṁ.

gold_answer: Verse 12.4 states that self is the lord of self, and with a subdued self one finds a rare master.
question: Which verse asks who else could be the lord of self, declaring that self is the lord of self?

hi.gold_answer: श्लोक 12.4 में कहा गया है कि स्वयं स्वयं का स्वामी है, और एक विनम्र आत्म के साथ व्यक्ति को एक दुर्लभ स्वामी मिलता है।
hi.question: कौन सा श्लोक पूछता है कि स्वयं का स्वामी और कौन हो सकता है, यह घोषणा करते हुए कि स्वयं स्वयं का स्वामी है?

pi.gold_answer: Gāthā 12.4 vadati yaṁ attā attano nātho, sudantena ca attanā dullabhaṁ nāthaṁ labhati.
pi.question: Katamā gāthā pucchati ko añño nātho siyā, vadati ca yaṁ attā hi attano nātho?

_gold_src.text_en: Self is the lord of self, who else could be the lord? With self well subdued, a man finds a lord such as few can find.
_gold_src.original: Attā hi attano nātho, ko hi nātho paro siyā; Attanā hi sudantena, nāthaṁ labhati dullabhaṁ.

_nearmiss_src.text_en: If a man make himself as he teaches others to be, then, being himself well subdued, he may subdue (others); one's own self is indeed difficult to subdue.
_nearmiss_src.original: Attānañce tathā kayirā, yathāññamanusāsati; Sudanto vata dametha, attā hi kira duddamo.

_nearmiss_src.text_en: By oneself the evil is done, by oneself one suffers; by oneself evil is left undone, by oneself one is purified. Purity and impurity belong to oneself, no one can purify another.
_nearmiss_src.original: Attanā hi kataṁ pāpaṁ, attanā saṅkilissati; Attanā akataṁ pāpaṁ, attanāva visujjhati; Suddhī asuddhi paccattaṁ, nāñño aññaṁ visodhaye.

gold_answer: Verse 14.5 gives the teaching of the Buddhas: avoid all sin, do good, purify the mind.
question: Which verse sums up the teaching of all the Awakened as: not to commit any sin, to do good, and to purify one's mind?

hi.gold_answer: श्लोक 14.5 बुद्धों की शिक्षा देता हैः सभी पापों से बचें, अच्छा करें, मन को शुद्ध करें।
hi.question: कौन सा श्लोक सभी जागृत लोगों की शिक्षा का सारांश देता हैः कोई पाप न करना, अच्छा करना और अपने मन को शुद्ध करना?

pi.gold_answer: Gāthā 14.5 buddhānaṁ sāsanaṁ deseti: sabbapāpassa akaraṇaṁ, kusalassa upasampadā, sacittapariyodapanaṁ.
pi.question: Katamā gāthā sabbesaṁ buddhānaṁ sāsanaṁ evaṁ saṅgaṇhāti: sabbapāpassa akaraṇaṁ, kusalassa upasampadā, sacittapariyodapanaṁ?

_gold_src.text_en: Not to commit any sin, to do good, and to purify one's mind, that is the teaching of (all) the Awakened.
_gold_src.original: Sabbapāpassa akaraṇaṁ, kusalassa upasampadā; Sacittapariyodapanaṁ, etaṁ buddhāna sāsanaṁ.

_nearmiss_src.text_en: The Awakened call patience the highest penance, long-suffering the highest Nirvana; for he is not an anchorite (pravragita) who strikes others, he is not an ascetic (sramana) who insults others.
_nearmiss_src.original: Khantī paramaṁ tapo titikkhā, Nibbānaṁ paramaṁ vadanti buddhā; Na hi pabbajito parūpaghātī, Na samaṇo hoti paraṁ viheṭhayanto.

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Not to blame, not to strike, to live restrained under the law, to be moderate in eating, to sleep and sit alone, and to dwell on the highest thoughts,—this is the teaching of the Awakened.
_nearmiss_src.original: Anūpavādo anūpaghāto, Pātimokkhe ca saṁvaro; Mattaññutā ca bhattasmiṁ, Pantañca sayanāsanaṁ; Adhicitte ca āyogo, Etaṁ buddhāna sāsanaṁ.

gold_answer: Verse 15.8 names health the greatest gift, contentment the best wealth, trust the best kin, and Nirvana the highest happiness.
question: Which verse calls health the greatest of gifts, contentment the best riches, and trust the best of relationships?

hi.gold_answer: श्लोक 15.8 में स्वास्थ्य को सबसे बड़ा उपहार, संतोष को सबसे अच्छा धन, सबसे अच्छे रिश्तेदारों पर भरोसा और निर्वाण को सबसे बड़ा सुख बताया गया है।
hi.question: कौन सा श्लोक स्वास्थ्य को सबसे बड़ा उपहार, संतुष्टि को सबसे अच्छा धन, और सबसे अच्छे संबंधों पर भरोसा करता है?

pi.gold_answer: Gāthā 15.8 ārogyaparamā lābhā, santuṭṭhiparamaṁ dhanaṁ, vissāsaparamā ñāti, nibbānaṁ paramaṁ sukhanti vadati.
pi.question: Katamā gāthā ārogyaṁ paramaṁ lābhaṁ, santuṭṭhiṁ paramaṁ dhanaṁ, vissāsaṁ ca paramaṁ ñātiṁ vadati?

_gold_src.text_en: Health is the greatest of gifts, contentedness the best riches; trust is the best of relationships, Nirvana the highest happiness.
_gold_src.original: Ārogyaparamā lābhā, Santuṭṭhiparamaṁ dhanaṁ; Vissāsaparamā ñāti, Nibbānaṁ paramaṁ sukhaṁ.

_nearmiss_src.text_en: There is no fire like passion; there is no losing throw like hatred; there is no pain like this body; there is no happiness higher than rest.
_nearmiss_src.original: Natthi rāgasamo aggi, Natthi dosasamo kali; Natthi khandhasamā dukkhā, Natthi santiparaṁ sukhaṁ.

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Hunger is the worst of diseases, the body the greatest of pains; if one knows this truly, that is Nirvana, the highest happiness.
_nearmiss_src.original: Jighacchāparamā rogā, saṅkhāraparamā dukhā; Etaṁ ñatvā yathābhūtaṁ, nibbānaṁ paramaṁ sukhaṁ.

gold_answer: Verse 17.3 advises overcoming anger by love, evil by good, the miser by giving, and the liar by truth.
question: Which verse instructs one to overcome anger by love, evil by good, the greedy by liberality, and the liar by truth?

hi.gold_answer: श्लोक 17.3 क्रोध पर प्रेम से, बुराई पर अच्छाई से, दुखी पर देने से और झूठे पर सच्चाई से काबू पाने की सलाह देता है।
hi.question: कौन सा श्लोक क्रोध को प्रेम से, बुराई को अच्छाई से, लालची को उदारता से और झूठे को सत्य से दूर करने का निर्देश देता है?

pi.gold_answer: Gāthā 17.3 vadati yaṁ akkodhena kodhaṁ jine, sādhunā asādhuṁ, dānena kadariyaṁ, saccena alikavādinaṁ.
pi.question: Katamā gāthā vadati yaṁ akkodhena kodhaṁ jine, sādhunā asādhuṁ, dānena kadariyaṁ, saccena ca alikavādinaṁ?

_gold_src.text_en: Let a man overcome anger by love, let him overcome evil by good; let him overcome the greedy by liberality, the liar by truth!
_gold_src.original: Akkodhena jine kodhaṁ, asādhuṁ sādhunā jine; Jine kadariyaṁ dānena, saccenālikavādinaṁ.

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Let a man leave anger, let him forsake pride, let him overcome all bondage! No sufferings befall the man who is not attached to name and form, and who calls nothing his own.
_nearmiss_src.original: Kodhaṁ jahe vippajaheyya mānaṁ, Saṁyojanaṁ sabbamatikkameyya; Taṁ nāmarūpasmimasajjamānaṁ, Akiñcanaṁ nānupatanti dukkhā.

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Speak the truth, do not yield to anger; give, if thou art asked for little; by these three steps thou wilt go near the gods.
_nearmiss_src.original: Saccaṁ bhaṇe na kujjheyya, dajjā appampi yācito; Etehi tīhi ṭhānehi, gacche devāna santike.

gold_answer: Verse 20.1 calls the eightfold path the best of ways and the four truths the best of truths.
question: Which verse names the eightfold path the best of ways and the four words the best of truths?

hi.gold_answer: श्लोक 20.1 आठ गुना मार्ग को सबसे अच्छा मार्ग और चार सत्यों को सबसे अच्छा सत्य कहता है।
hi.question: कौन सा श्लोक आठ गुना मार्ग को सबसे अच्छा मार्ग और चार शब्दों को सबसे अच्छा सत्य नाम देता है?

pi.gold_answer: Gāthā 20.1 aṭṭhaṅgikaṁ maggaṁ maggānaṁ seṭṭhaṁ, cattāri ca saccāni saccānaṁ seṭṭhāni vadati.
pi.question: Katamā gāthā aṭṭhaṅgikaṁ maggaṁ maggānaṁ seṭṭhaṁ, caturo ca pade saccānaṁ seṭṭhe vadati?

_gold_src.text_en: The best of ways is the eightfold; the best of truths the four words; the best of virtues passionlessness; the best of men he who has eyes to see.
_gold_src.original: Maggānaṭṭhaṅgiko seṭṭho, saccānaṁ caturo padā; Virāgo seṭṭho dhammānaṁ, dvipadānañca cakkhumā.

_nearmiss_src.text_en: This is the way, there is no other that leads to the purifying of intelligence. Go on this way! Everything else is the deceit of Mara (the tempter).
_nearmiss_src.original: Eseva maggo natthañño, Dassanassa visuddhiyā; Etañhi tumhe paṭipajjatha, Mārassetaṁ pamohanaṁ.

_nearmiss_src.text_en: `All created things perish,' he who knows and sees this becomes passive in pain; this is the way to purity.
_nearmiss_src.original: “Sabbe saṅkhārā aniccā”ti, yadā paññāya passati; Atha nibbindati dukkhe, esa maggo visuddhiyā.

gold_answer: Verse 1.13 likens passion entering an unreflecting mind to rain leaking through an ill-thatched roof.
question: Which passage compares passion breaking into an unreflecting mind to rain breaking through a poorly thatched house?

hi.gold_answer: श्लोक 1.13 एक प्रतिबिंबित मन में प्रवेश करने वाले जुनून की तुलना एक खराब छत से रिसने वाली बारिश से करता है।
hi.question: कौन सा अंश एक विचारहीन मन में टूटने के जुनून की तुलना एक खराब छप्पर वाले घर में बारिश के टूटने से करता है?

pi.gold_answer: Gāthā 1.13 rāgaṁ abhāvitaṁ cittaṁ pavisantaṁ vuṭṭhiṁ ducchannaṁ agāraṁ samativijjhantiṁ viya upameti.
pi.question: Katamo passo rāgaṁ abhāvitaṁ cittaṁ samativijjhantaṁ vuṭṭhiyā ducchannaṁ agāraṁ samativijjhantiyā upameti?

_gold_src.text_en: As rain breaks through an ill-thatched house, passion will break through an unreflecting mind.
_gold_src.original: Yathā agāraṁ ducchannaṁ, vuṭṭhī samativijjhati; Evaṁ abhāvitaṁ cittaṁ, rāgo samativijjhati.

_nearmiss_src.text_en: As rain does not break through a well-thatched house, passion will not break through a well-reflecting mind.
_nearmiss_src.original: Yathā agāraṁ suchannaṁ, vuṭṭhī na samativijjhati; Evaṁ subhāvitaṁ cittaṁ, rāgo na samativijjhati.

_nearmiss_src.text_en: He who lives looking for pleasures only, his senses uncontrolled, immoderate in his food, idle, and weak, Mara (the tempter) will certainly overthrow him, as the wind throws down a weak tree.
_nearmiss_src.original: Subhānupassiṁ viharantaṁ, indriyesu asaṁvutaṁ; Bhojanamhi cāmattaññuṁ, kusītaṁ hīnavīriyaṁ; Taṁ ve pasahati māro, vāto rukkhaṁva dubbalaṁ.

gold_answer: Verse 2.5 says the wise man builds for himself, through earnestness, an island that no flood overwhelms.
question: Which passage says that by rousing himself, by earnestness and restraint, the wise man may make for himself an island no flood can overwhelm?

hi.gold_answer: श्लोक 2.5 में कहा गया है कि बुद्धिमान व्यक्ति अपने लिए, लगन के माध्यम से, एक ऐसा द्वीप बनाता है जहाँ कोई बाढ़ नहीं आती है।
hi.question: कौन सा अंश कहता है कि ईमानदारी और संयम से खुद को उत्तेजित करके, बुद्धिमान व्यक्ति अपने लिए एक ऐसा द्वीप बना सकता है जिस पर कोई भी बाढ़ हावी नहीं हो सकती है?

pi.gold_answer: Gāthā 2.5 vadati yaṁ medhāvī appamādena attano dīpaṁ karoti yaṁ ogho na abhikīrati.
pi.question: Katamo passo vadati yaṁ uṭṭhānena appamādena saṁyamena ca medhāvī attano dīpaṁ kareyya yaṁ ogho na abhikīrati?

_gold_src.text_en: By rousing himself, by earnestness, by restraint and control, the wise man may make for himself an island which no flood can overwhelm.
_gold_src.original: Uṭṭhānenappamādena, saṁyamena damena ca; Dīpaṁ kayirātha medhāvī, yaṁ ogho nābhikīrati.

_nearmiss_src.text_en: If an earnest person has roused himself, if he is not forgetful, if his deeds are pure, if he acts with consideration, if he restrains himself, and lives according to law,—then his glory will increase.
_nearmiss_src.original: Uṭṭhānavato satīmato, Sucikammassa nisammakārino; Saññatassa dhammajīvino, Appamattassa yasobhivaḍḍhati.

_nearmiss_src.text_en: These wise people, meditative, steady, always possessed of strong powers, attain to Nirvana, the highest happiness.
_nearmiss_src.original: Te jhāyino sātatikā, niccaṁ daḷhaparakkamā; Phusanti dhīrā nibbānaṁ, yogakkhemaṁ anuttaraṁ.

gold_answer: Verse 3.8 tells the seeker to fortify the mind and assail Mara with the weapon of knowledge.
question: Which passage urges one to make the mind firm like a fortress, knowing the body is fragile like a jar, and to attack Mara with the weapon of knowledge?

hi.gold_answer: श्लोक 3.8 साधक को मन को मजबूत करने और ज्ञान के हथियार से मारा पर हमला करने के लिए कहता है।
hi.question: कौन सा अंश मन को एक किले की तरह दृढ़ बनाने के लिए प्रेरित करता है, यह जानते हुए कि शरीर एक जार की तरह नाजुक है, और ज्ञान के हथियार से मारा पर हमला करने के लिए?

pi.gold_answer: Gāthā 3.8 vadati yaṁ cittaṁ nagaraṁ viya thiraṁ katvā paññāvudhena māraṁ yodhetha.
pi.question: Katamo passo vadati yaṁ imaṁ kāyaṁ kumbhūpamaṁ viditvā cittaṁ nagarūpamaṁ ṭhapetvā paññāvudhena māraṁ yodhetha?

_gold_src.text_en: Knowing that this body is (fragile) like a jar, and making this thought firm like a fortress, one should attack Mara (the tempter) with the weapon of knowledge, one should watch him when conquered, and should never rest.
_gold_src.original: Kumbhūpamaṁ kāyamimaṁ viditvā, Nagarūpamaṁ cittamidaṁ ṭhapetvā; Yodhetha māraṁ paññāvudhena, Jitañca rakkhe anivesano siyā.

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Before long, alas! this body will lie on the earth, despised, without understanding, like a useless log.
_nearmiss_src.original: Aciraṁ vatayaṁ kāyo, pathaviṁ adhisessati; Chuddho apetaviññāṇo, niratthaṁva kaliṅgaraṁ.

_nearmiss_src.text_en: As a fish taken from his watery home and thrown on dry ground, our thought trembles all over in order to escape the dominion of Mara (the tempter).
_nearmiss_src.original: Vārijova thale khitto, okamokataubbhato; Pariphandatidaṁ cittaṁ, māradheyyaṁ pahātave.

gold_answer: Verse 3.9 foretells that the body will soon lie on the ground, cast aside like a useless log.
question: Which passage says that before long this body will lie on the earth, despised and useless like a discarded log?

hi.gold_answer: श्लोक 3.9 भविष्यवाणी करता है कि शरीर जल्द ही जमीन पर लेट जाएगा, एक बेकार लकड़ी की तरह एक तरफ फेंक दिया जाएगा।
hi.question: कौन सा अंश कहता है कि जल्द ही यह शरीर पृथ्वी पर पड़ा रहेगा, एक फेंके हुए लट्ठ की तरह तुच्छ और बेकार?

pi.gold_answer: Gāthā 3.9 vadati yaṁ ayaṁ kāyo aciraṁ pathaviyaṁ sessati, chuddho apetaviññāṇo niratthaṁ kaliṅgaraṁ viya.
pi.question: Katamo passo vadati yaṁ aciraṁ ayaṁ kāyo pathaviṁ adhisessati, chuddho niratthaṁ kaliṅgaraṁ viya?

_gold_src.text_en: Before long, alas! this body will lie on the earth, despised, without understanding, like a useless log.
_gold_src.original: Aciraṁ vatayaṁ kāyo, pathaviṁ adhisessati; Chuddho apetaviññāṇo, niratthaṁva kaliṅgaraṁ.

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Knowing that this body is (fragile) like a jar, and making this thought firm like a fortress, one should attack Mara (the tempter) with the weapon of knowledge, one should watch him when conquered, and should never rest.
_nearmiss_src.original: Kumbhūpamaṁ kāyamimaṁ viditvā, Nagarūpamaṁ cittamidaṁ ṭhapetvā; Yodhetha māraṁ paññāvudhena, Jitañca rakkhe anivesano siyā.

_nearmiss_src.text_en: As a fletcher makes straight his arrow, a wise man makes straight his trembling and unsteady thought, which is difficult to guard, difficult to hold back.
_nearmiss_src.original: Phandanaṁ capalaṁ cittaṁ, dūrakkhaṁ dunnivārayaṁ; Ujuṁ karoti medhāvī, usukārova tejanaṁ.

gold_answer: Verse 5.4 says the fool aware of his folly is wise to that extent, but one who deems himself wise is truly a fool.
question: Which passage says the fool who knows his own foolishness is so far wise, while the fool who thinks himself wise is a fool indeed?

hi.gold_answer: श्लोक 5.4 में कहा गया है कि जो मूर्ख अपनी मूर्खता से अवगत होता है वह उस हद तक बुद्धिमान होता है, लेकिन जो खुद को बुद्धिमान समझता है वह वास्तव में मूर्ख होता है।
hi.question: कौन सा अंश कहता है कि मूर्ख जो अपनी मूर्खता को जानता है वह अब तक बुद्धिमान है, जबकि मूर्ख जो खुद को बुद्धिमान समझता है वह वास्तव में मूर्ख है?

pi.gold_answer: Gāthā 5.4 vadati yaṁ bālo attano bālyaṁ jānanto tattakena paṇḍito, paṇḍitamānī pana bālo eva vuccati.
pi.question: Katamo passo vadati yaṁ yo bālo attano bālyaṁ jānāti so tāvatā paṇḍito, yo pana paṇḍitamānī so ve bālo?

_gold_src.text_en: The fool who knows his foolishness, is wise at least so far. But a fool who thinks himself wise, he is called a fool indeed.
_gold_src.original: Yo bālo maññati bālyaṁ, paṇḍito vāpi tena so; Bālo ca paṇḍitamānī, sa ve “bālo”ti vuccati.

_nearmiss_src.text_en: "These sons belong to me, and this wealth belongs to me," with such thoughts a fool is tormented. He himself does not belong to himself; how much less sons and wealth?
_nearmiss_src.original: Puttā matthi dhanaṁ matthi, iti bālo vihaññati; Attā hi attano natthi, kuto puttā kuto dhanaṁ.

_nearmiss_src.text_en: If a fool be associated with a wise man even all his life, he will perceive the truth as little as a spoon perceives the taste of soup.
_nearmiss_src.original: Yāvajīvampi ce bālo, paṇḍitaṁ payirupāsati; Na so dhammaṁ vijānāti, dabbī sūparasaṁ yathā.

gold_answer: Verse 5.5 compares a fool beside a wise man to a spoon that never tastes the soup.
question: Which passage says a fool may live his whole life with a wise man yet perceive the truth no more than a spoon tastes soup?

hi.gold_answer: श्लोक 5.5 एक बुद्धिमान व्यक्ति के बगल में एक मूर्ख की तुलना एक ऐसे चम्मच से करता है जो कभी भी सूप का स्वाद नहीं लेता है।
hi.question: कौन सा अंश कहता है कि एक मूर्ख अपना पूरा जीवन एक बुद्धिमान व्यक्ति के साथ जी सकता है, फिर भी सच्चाई को एक चम्मच के स्वाद वाले सूप से ज्यादा नहीं समझता है?

pi.gold_answer: Gāthā 5.5 bālaṁ paṇḍitassa santike, dabbiṁ yā sūparasaṁ na jānāti, viya upameti.
pi.question: Katamo passo vadati yaṁ bālo yāvajīvampi paṇḍitaṁ payirupāsanto dhammaṁ na vijānāti, dabbī sūparasaṁ viya?

_gold_src.text_en: If a fool be associated with a wise man even all his life, he will perceive the truth as little as a spoon perceives the taste of soup.
_gold_src.original: Yāvajīvampi ce bālo, paṇḍitaṁ payirupāsati; Na so dhammaṁ vijānāti, dabbī sūparasaṁ yathā.

_nearmiss_src.text_en: If an intelligent man be associated for one minute only with a wise man, he will soon perceive the truth, as the tongue perceives the taste of soup.
_nearmiss_src.original: Muhuttamapi ce viññū, paṇḍitaṁ payirupāsati; Khippaṁ dhammaṁ vijānāti, jivhā sūparasaṁ yathā.

_nearmiss_src.text_en: The fool who knows his foolishness, is wise at least so far. But a fool who thinks himself wise, he is called a fool indeed.
_nearmiss_src.original: Yo bālo maññati bālyaṁ, paṇḍito vāpi tena so; Bālo ca paṇḍitamānī, sa ve “bālo”ti vuccati.

gold_answer: Verse 6.6 says the wise, like a rock unmoved by wind, are not shaken by blame or praise.
question: Which passage compares the steadiness of the wise amid blame and praise to a solid rock unshaken by the wind?

hi.gold_answer: श्लोक 6.6 में कहा गया है कि बुद्धिमान, हवा से हिलती चट्टान की तरह, दोष या प्रशंसा से हिलते नहीं हैं।
hi.question: कौन सा अंश दोष और प्रशंसा के बीच बुद्धिमान की स्थिरता की तुलना हवा से बिना हिलाये एक ठोस चट्टान से करता है?

pi.gold_answer: Gāthā 6.6 vadati yaṁ paṇḍitā ekaghano selo viya vātena akampito nindāya pasaṁsāya ca na samiñjanti.
pi.question: Katamo passo paṇḍitānaṁ nindāpasaṁsāsu thiratañ ca selaṁ ekaghanaṁ vātena akampitaṁ viya upameti?

_gold_src.text_en: As a solid rock is not shaken by the wind, wise people falter not amidst blame and praise.
_gold_src.original: Selo yathā ekaghano, vātena na samīrati; Evaṁ nindāpasaṁsāsu, na samiñjanti paṇḍitā.

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Wise people, after they have listened to the laws, become serene, like a deep, smooth, and still lake.
_nearmiss_src.original: Yathāpi rahado gambhīro, vippasanno anāvilo; Evaṁ dhammāni sutvāna, vippasīdanti paṇḍitā.

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Good people walk on whatever befall, the good do not prattle, longing for pleasure; whether touched by happiness or sorrow wise people never appear elated or depressed.
_nearmiss_src.original: Sabbattha ve sappurisā cajanti, Na kāmakāmā lapayanti santo; Sukhena phuṭṭhā atha vā dukhena, Na uccāvacaṁ paṇḍitā dassayanti.

gold_answer: Verse 6.3 advises against friendship with the wicked and in favour of the virtuous.
question: Which passage warns against making evil-doers or low people one's friends and urges befriending the virtuous and the best of men?

hi.gold_answer: श्लोक 6.3 दुष्टों के साथ मित्रता के विरुद्ध और गुणी लोगों के पक्ष में सलाह देता है।
hi.question: कौन सा अंश दुष्टों या निम्न लोगों को अपना मित्र बनाने के खिलाफ चेतावनी देता है और गुणी और सर्वश्रेष्ठ लोगों से दोस्ती करने का आग्रह करता है?

pi.gold_answer: Gāthā 6.3 vadati yaṁ pāpake mitte na bhaje, kalyāṇe pana mitte purisuttame ca bhajetha.
pi.question: Katamo passo vadati yaṁ na pāpake mitte na purisādhame bhaje, kalyāṇe pana mitte purisuttame ca bhajetha?

_gold_src.text_en: Do not have evil-doers for friends, do not have low people for friends: have virtuous people for friends, have for friends the best of men.
_gold_src.original: Na bhaje pāpake mitte, na bhaje purisādhame; Bhajetha mitte kalyāṇe, bhajetha purisuttame.

_nearmiss_src.text_en: If you see an intelligent man who tells you where true treasures are to be found, who shows what is to be avoided, and administers reproofs, follow that wise man; it will be better, not worse, for those who follow him.
_nearmiss_src.original: Nidhīnaṁva pavattāraṁ, yaṁ passe vajjadassinaṁ; Niggayhavādiṁ medhāviṁ, tādisaṁ paṇḍitaṁ bhaje; Tādisaṁ bhajamānassa, seyyo hoti na pāpiyo.

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Let him admonish, let him teach, let him forbid what is improper!—he will be beloved of the good, by the bad he will be hated.
_nearmiss_src.original: Ovadeyyānusāseyya, asabbhā ca nivāraye; Satañhi so piyo hoti, asataṁ hoti appiyo.

gold_answer: Verse 8.11 prefers one virtuous, reflective day to a hundred vicious, unrestrained years.
question: Which passage says a single day lived virtuous and reflecting is better than a hundred years lived vicious and unrestrained?

hi.gold_answer: श्लोक 8.11 सौ दुष्ट, अनियंत्रित वर्षों के बजाय एक पुण्यपूर्ण, चिंतनशील दिन को पसंद करता है।
hi.question: कौन सा अंश कहता है कि एक दिन सद्गुणी और चिंतनशील जीवन जीना एक सौ साल दुष्ट और अनियंत्रित जीवन जीने से बेहतर है?

pi.gold_answer: Gāthā 8.11 vadati yaṁ sīlavato jhāyino ekāhaṁ jīvitaṁ dussīlassa asamāhitassa vassasatā seyyo.
pi.question: Katamo passo vadati yaṁ sīlavato jhāyino ekāhaṁ jīvitaṁ seyyo yaṁ dussīlassa asamāhitassa vassasataṁ jīvitaṁ?

_gold_src.text_en: But he who lives a hundred years, vicious and unrestrained, a life of one day is better if a man is virtuous and reflecting.
_gold_src.original: Yo ca vassasataṁ jīve, dussīlo asamāhito; Ekāhaṁ jīvitaṁ seyyo, sīlavantassa jhāyino.

_nearmiss_src.text_en: And he who lives a hundred years, ignorant and unrestrained, a life of one day is better if a man is wise and reflecting.
_nearmiss_src.original: Yo ca vassasataṁ jīve, duppañño asamāhito; Ekāhaṁ jīvitaṁ seyyo, paññavantassa jhāyino.

_nearmiss_src.text_en: And he who lives a hundred years, idle and weak, a life of one day is better if a man has attained firm strength.
_nearmiss_src.original: Yo ca vassasataṁ jīve, kusīto hīnavīriyo; Ekāhaṁ jīvitaṁ seyyo, vīriyamārabhato daḷhaṁ.

gold_answer: Verse 9.12 says nowhere—sky, sea, or mountains—can one flee an evil deed.
question: Which passage says there is no spot in sky, sea, or mountain cleft where one can escape the consequence of an evil deed?

hi.gold_answer: आयत 9.12 में कहा गया है कि कहीं भी-आकाश, समुद्र या पहाड़-कोई किसी बुरे काम से भाग नहीं सकता है।
hi.question: कौन सा परिच्छेद कहता है कि आकाश, समुद्र या पहाड़ के दरार में ऐसा कोई स्थान नहीं है जहाँ कोई व्यक्ति किसी बुरे कर्म के परिणाम से बच सके?

pi.gold_answer: Gāthā 9.12 vadati yaṁ na antalikkhe na samudde na pabbatavivare koci padeso yattha ṭhito pāpakammā mucceyya.
pi.question: Katamo passo vadati yaṁ na antalikkhe na samuddamajjhe na pabbatānaṁ vivare so jagatippadeso vijjati yattha ṭhito pāpakammā mucceyya?

_gold_src.text_en: Not in the sky, not in the midst of the sea, not if we enter into the clefts of the mountains, is there known a spot in the whole world where a man might be freed from an evil deed.
_gold_src.original: Na antalikkhe na samuddamajjhe, Na pabbatānaṁ vivaraṁ pavissa; Na vijjatī so jagatippadeso, Yatthaṭṭhito mucceyya pāpakammā.

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Not in the sky, not in the midst of the sea, not if we enter into the clefts of the mountains, is there known a spot in the whole world where death could not overcome (the mortal).
_nearmiss_src.original: Na antalikkhe na samuddamajjhe, Na pabbatānaṁ vivaraṁ pavissa; Na vijjatī so jagatippadeso, Yatthaṭṭhitaṁ nappasaheyya maccu. Pāpavaggo navamo.

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Some people are born again; evil-doers go to hell; righteous people go to heaven; those who are free from all worldly desires attain Nirvana.
_nearmiss_src.original: Gabbhameke uppajjanti, nirayaṁ pāpakammino; Saggaṁ sugatino yanti, parinibbanti anāsavā.

gold_answer: Verse 13.4 advises viewing the world as a bubble and mirage, escaping the king of death's notice.
question: Which passage tells the reader to look on the world as a bubble or a mirage, so that the king of death will not see him?

hi.gold_answer: श्लोक 13.4 दुनिया को एक बुलबुला और मृगजळ के रूप में देखने की सलाह देता है, जो मृत्यु के राजा के ध्यान से बच जाता है।
hi.question: कौन सा अंश पाठक को दुनिया को एक बुलबुला या एक मृग के रूप में देखने के लिए कहता है, ताकि मृत्यु का राजा उसे न देखे?

pi.gold_answer: Gāthā 13.4 vadati yaṁ lokaṁ pubbuḷaṁ marīcikañca viya avekkhantaṁ maccurājā na passati.
pi.question: Katamo passo vadati yaṁ lokaṁ pubbuḷakaṁ viya marīcikaṁ viya passeyya, yathā maccurājā na passati?

_gold_src.text_en: Look upon the world as a bubble, look upon it as a mirage: the king of death does not see him who thus looks down upon the world.
_gold_src.original: Yathā pubbuḷakaṁ passe, yathā passe marīcikaṁ; Evaṁ lokaṁ avekkhantaṁ, maccurājā na passati.

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Come, look at this glittering world, like unto a royal chariot; the foolish are immersed in it, but the wise do not touch it.
_nearmiss_src.original: Etha passathimaṁ lokaṁ, cittaṁ rājarathūpamaṁ; Yattha bālā visīdanti, natthi saṅgo vijānataṁ.

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Do not follow the evil law! Do not live on in thoughtlessness! Do not follow false doctrine! Be not a friend of the world.
_nearmiss_src.original: Hīnaṁ dhammaṁ na seveyya, pamādena na saṁvase; Micchādiṭṭhiṁ na seveyya, na siyā lokavaḍḍhano.

gold_answer: Verse 14.12 describes refuge in the Buddha, Law, and Church and clear sight of the four truths.
question: Which passage describes taking refuge in the Buddha, the Law, and the Church, and seeing the four holy truths?

hi.gold_answer: श्लोक 14.12 बुद्ध, कानून और चर्च में शरण और चार सत्यों की स्पष्ट दृष्टि का वर्णन करता है।
hi.question: कौन सा अंश बुद्ध, कानून और चर्च में शरण लेने और चार पवित्र सत्यों को देखने का वर्णन करता है?

pi.gold_answer: Gāthā 14.12 vaṇṇeti yaṁ buddhaṁ dhammaṁ saṅghaṁ saraṇaṁ gato cattāri ariyasaccāni sammappaññāya passati.
pi.question: Katamo passo vaṇṇeti yaṁ buddhaṁ dhammaṁ saṅghañca saraṇaṁ gantvā cattāri ariyasaccāni sammappaññāya passati?

_gold_src.text_en: He who takes refuge with Buddha, the Law, and the Church; he who, with clear understanding, sees the four holy truths:—
_gold_src.original: Yo ca buddhañca dhammañca, saṅghañca saraṇaṁ gato; Cattāri ariyasaccāni, sammappaññāya passati.

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Men, driven by fear, go to many a refuge, to mountains and forests, to groves and sacred trees.
_nearmiss_src.original: Bahuṁ ve saraṇaṁ yanti, pabbatāni vanāni ca; Ārāmarukkhacetyāni, manussā bhayatajjitā.

_nearmiss_src.text_en: But that is not a safe refuge, that is not the best refuge; a man is not delivered from all pains after having gone to that refuge.
_nearmiss_src.original: Netaṁ kho saraṇaṁ khemaṁ, netaṁ saraṇamuttamaṁ; Netaṁ saraṇamāgamma, sabbadukkhā pamuccati.

gold_answer: Verse 16.4 teaches that grief and fear spring from pleasure, and freedom from pleasure ends both.
question: Which passage says that from pleasure come grief and fear, and that one free from pleasure knows neither?

hi.gold_answer: श्लोक 16.4 सिखाता है कि दुःख और भय सुख से उत्पन्न होते हैं, और सुख से मुक्ति दोनों का अंत करती है।
hi.question: कौन सा अंश कहता है कि सुख से दुःख और भय आते हैं, और सुख से मुक्त व्यक्ति न तो जानता है?

pi.gold_answer: Gāthā 16.4 vadati yaṁ piyato soko ca bhayañca jāyati, piyato vippamuttassa pana neva soko na bhayaṁ.
pi.question: Katamo passo vadati yaṁ piyato soko bhayañca jāyati, piyato ca vippamuttassa natthi soko kuto bhayaṁ?

_gold_src.text_en: From pleasure comes grief, from pleasure comes fear; he who is free from pleasure knows neither grief nor fear.
_gold_src.original: Piyato jāyatī soko, piyato jāyatī bhayaṁ; Piyato vippamuttassa, natthi soko kuto bhayaṁ.

_nearmiss_src.text_en: From affection comes grief, from affection comes fear; he who is free from affection knows neither grief nor fear.
_nearmiss_src.original: Pemato jāyatī soko, pemato jāyatī bhayaṁ; Pemato vippamuttassa, natthi soko kuto bhayaṁ.

_nearmiss_src.text_en: From lust comes grief, from lust comes fear; he who is free from lust knows neither grief nor fear.
_nearmiss_src.original: Ratiyā jāyatī soko, ratiyā jāyatī bhayaṁ; Ratiyā vippamuttassa, natthi soko kuto bhayaṁ.

gold_answer: Verse 23.1 resolves to bear abuse silently as a battle-elephant endures arrows.
question: Which passage says one should silently endure abuse as the elephant in battle endures the arrow shot from the bow?

hi.gold_answer: श्लोक 23.1 दुर्व्यवहार को चुपचाप सहन करने का संकल्प करता है क्योंकि एक युद्ध-हाथी तीरों को सहन करता है।
hi.question: किस परिच्छेद में कहा गया है कि किसी को चुपचाप दुर्व्यवहार सहना चाहिए क्योंकि युद्ध में हाथी धनुष से तीर की गोली को सहता है?

pi.gold_answer: Gāthā 23.1 vadati yaṁ ahaṁ nāgo viya saṅgāme cāpato patitaṁ saraṁ ativākyaṁ titikkhissaṁ.
pi.question: Katamo passo vadati yaṁ nāgo viya saṅgāme cāpato patitaṁ saraṁ, tathā ativākyaṁ tuṇhī titikkheyya?

_gold_src.text_en: Silently shall I endure abuse as the elephant in battle endures the arrow sent from the bow: for the world is ill-natured.
_gold_src.original: Ahaṁ nāgova saṅgāme, cāpato patitaṁ saraṁ; Ativākyaṁ titikkhissaṁ, dussīlo hi bahujjano.

_nearmiss_src.text_en: They lead a tamed elephant to battle, the king mounts a tamed elephant; the tamed is the best among men, he who silently endures abuse.
_nearmiss_src.original: Dantaṁ nayanti samitiṁ, dantaṁ rājābhirūhati; Danto seṭṭho manussesu, yotivākyaṁ titikkhati.

_nearmiss_src.text_en: The elephant called Dhanapalaka, his temples running with sap, and difficult to hold, does not eat a morsel when bound; the elephant longs for the elephant grove.
_nearmiss_src.original: Dhanapālo nāma kuñjaro, Kaṭukabhedano dunnivārayo; Baddho kabaḷaṁ na bhuñjati, Sumarati nāgavanassa kuñjaro.

gold_answer: Verse 24.1 likens a heedless man's spreading thirst, leaping life to life, to a monkey after fruit.
question: Which passage compares the growing thirst of a thoughtless man, running from life to life, to a monkey seeking fruit in the forest?

hi.gold_answer: श्लोक 24.1 एक लापरवाह व्यक्ति की प्यास फैलाने, जीवन को जीने के लिए छलांग लगाने, की तुलना फलों के बाद एक बंदर से करता है।
hi.question: कौन सा अंश जीवन से जीवन की ओर दौड़ने वाले एक विचारहीन व्यक्ति की बढ़ती प्यास की तुलना जंगल में फल की तलाश करने वाले बंदर से करता है?

pi.gold_answer: Gāthā 24.1 pamattassa vaḍḍhamānaṁ taṇhaṁ, jātito jātiṁ plavantaṁ, vane phalaṁ icchantaṁ vānaraṁ viya upameti.
pi.question: Katamo passo pamattacārino taṇhaṁ vaḍḍhamānaṁ, hurā huraṁ plavantaṁ, vane phalaṁ icchantaṁ vānaraṁ viya upameti?

_gold_src.text_en: The thirst of a thoughtless man grows like a creeper; he runs from life to life, like a monkey seeking fruit in the forest.
_gold_src.original: Manujassa pamattacārino, Taṇhā vaḍḍhati māluvā viya; So plavatī hurā huraṁ, Phalamicchaṁva vanasmi vānaro.

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Whomsoever this fierce thirst overcomes, full of poison, in this world, his sufferings increase like the abounding Birana grass.
_nearmiss_src.original: Yaṁ esā sahate jammī, taṇhā loke visattikā; Sokā tassa pavaḍḍhanti, abhivaṭṭhaṁva bīraṇaṁ.

_nearmiss_src.text_en: He who overcomes this fierce thirst, difficult to be conquered in this world, sufferings fall off from him, like water-drops from a lotus leaf.
_nearmiss_src.original: Yo cetaṁ sahate jammiṁ, taṇhaṁ loke duraccayaṁ; Sokā tamhā papatanti, udabinduva pokkharā.

gold_answer: Hatred persists in those who nurse thoughts of injury (1.3) and ceases in those who do not (1.4); it is never quenched by hatred but only by love (1.5).
question: How does the Dhammapada describe the way hatred is sustained and the way it is finally stilled?

hi.gold_answer: घृणा उन लोगों में बनी रहती है जो चोट के विचारों को पालते हैं (1.3) और उन लोगों में समाप्त हो जाती है जो नहीं करते हैं (1.4); यह कभी भी घृणा से नहीं बल्कि केवल प्रेम से बुझती है (1.5)।
hi.question: धम्मपद किस तरह से घृणा को बनाए रखा जाता है और अंत में इसे कैसे शांत किया जाता है, इसका वर्णन कैसे करता है?

pi.gold_answer: Veraṁ tesu na sammati ye 'akkocchi maṁ' ti upanayhanti (1.3), tesu ca sammati ye na upanayhanti (1.4); verena kadācanaṁ na sammati, averena pana sammati (1.5).
pi.question: Kathaṁ Dhammapadaṁ vaṇṇeti yena veraṁ upanayhati ca yena ca ante sammati?

_gold_src.text_en: "He abused me, he beat me, he defeated me, he robbed me,"—in those who harbour such thoughts hatred will never cease.
_gold_src.original: Akkocchi maṁ avadhi maṁ, ajini maṁ ahāsi me; Ye ca taṁ upanayhanti, veraṁ tesaṁ na sammati.

_gold_src.text_en: "He abused me, he beat me, he defeated me, he robbed me,"—in those who do not harbour such thoughts hatred will cease.
_gold_src.original: Akkocchi maṁ avadhi maṁ, ajini maṁ ahāsi me; Ye ca taṁ nupanayhanti, veraṁ tesūpasammati.

_gold_src.text_en: For hatred does not cease by hatred at any time: hatred ceases by love, this is an old rule.
_gold_src.original: Na hi verena verāni, sammantīdha kudācanaṁ; Averena ca sammanti, esa dhammo sanantano.

_nearmiss_src.text_en: The world does not know that we must all come to an end here;—but those who know it, their quarrels cease at once.
_nearmiss_src.original: Pare ca na vijānanti, mayamettha yamāmase; Ye ca tattha vijānanti, tato sammanti medhagā.

gold_answer: We are the product of our thoughts: an evil thought brings pain like a wheel after the ox (1.1), while a pure thought brings happiness like an unleaving shadow (1.2).
question: What does the Dhammapada teach about how thought shapes a person's experience of suffering and happiness?

hi.gold_answer: हम अपने विचारों की उपज हैंः एक बुरा विचार बैल के बाद एक चक्र की तरह दर्द लाता है (1.1), जबकि एक शुद्ध विचार एक निर्बाध छाया की तरह खुशी लाता है (1.2)।
hi.question: धम्मपद इस बारे में क्या सिखाता है कि विचार किसी व्यक्ति के दुख और खुशी के अनुभव को कैसे आकार देते हैं?

pi.gold_answer: Mayaṁ manomayā homa: paduṭṭhaṁ manaṁ dukkhaṁ anveti cakkaṁ viya vahato padaṁ (1.1), pasannaṁ pana manaṁ sukhaṁ anveti chāyā viya anapāyinī (1.2).
pi.question: Kiṁ Dhammapadaṁ deseti yaṁ mano purisassa dukkhasukhānubhavaṁ kathaṁ niyameti?

_gold_src.text_en: All that we are is the result of what we have thought: it is founded on our thoughts, it is made up of our thoughts. If a man speaks or acts with an evil thought, pain follows him, as the wheel follows the foot of the ox that draws the carriage.
_gold_src.original: Manopubbaṅgamā dhammā, manoseṭṭhā manomayā; Manasā ce paduṭṭhena, bhāsati vā karoti vā; Tato naṁ dukkhamanveti, cakkaṁva vahato padaṁ.

_gold_src.text_en: All that we are is the result of what we have thought: it is founded on our thoughts, it is made up of our thoughts. If a man speaks or acts with a pure thought, happiness follows him, like a shadow that never leaves him.
_gold_src.original: Manopubbaṅgamā dhammā, manoseṭṭhā manomayā; Manasā ce pasannena, bhāsati vā karoti vā; Tato naṁ sukhamanveti, chāyāva anapāyinī.

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Let the wise man guard his thoughts, for they are difficult to perceive, very artful, and they rush wherever they list: thoughts well guarded bring happiness.
_nearmiss_src.original: Sududdasaṁ sunipuṇaṁ, yatthakāmanipātinaṁ; Cittaṁ rakkhetha medhāvī, cittaṁ guttaṁ sukhāvahaṁ.

gold_answer: Mara overthrows the pleasure-seeker of uncontrolled senses like wind felling a weak tree (1.7), but cannot move the controlled and faithful man, like wind against a rocky mountain (1.8).
question: How does the text contrast the man who chases pleasures with the man who is self-controlled, in terms of Mara's power over him?

hi.gold_answer: मारा अनियंत्रित इंद्रियों के आनंद चाहने वाले को उखाड़ फेंकता है जैसे हवा एक कमजोर पेड़ को काटती है (1.7), लेकिन नियंत्रित और वफादार आदमी को एक चट्टानी पहाड़ (1.8) के खिलाफ हवा की तरह नहीं हिला सकता है।
hi.question: पाठ उस व्यक्ति की तुलना उस व्यक्ति से कैसे करता है जो आनंद का पीछा करता है और उस व्यक्ति की तुलना उस व्यक्ति से करता है जो उस पर मारा की शक्ति के संदर्भ में आत्म-नियंत्रित है?

pi.gold_answer: Māro subhānupassiṁ asaṁvutindriyaṁ pasahati vāto dubbalaṁ rukkhaṁ viya (1.7), saṁvutaṁ pana saddhaṁ purisaṁ na pasahati vāto selaṁ pabbataṁ viya (1.8).
pi.question: Kathaṁ pāḷi subhānupassiṁ purisaṁ saṁvutindriyena purisena, mārassa balena, viseseti?

_gold_src.text_en: He who lives looking for pleasures only, his senses uncontrolled, immoderate in his food, idle, and weak, Mara (the tempter) will certainly overthrow him, as the wind throws down a weak tree.
_gold_src.original: Subhānupassiṁ viharantaṁ, indriyesu asaṁvutaṁ; Bhojanamhi cāmattaññuṁ, kusītaṁ hīnavīriyaṁ; Taṁ ve pasahati māro, vāto rukkhaṁva dubbalaṁ.

_gold_src.text_en: He who lives without looking for pleasures, his senses well controlled, moderate in his food, faithful and strong, him Mara will certainly not overthrow, any more than the wind throws down a rocky mountain.
_gold_src.original: Asubhānupassiṁ viharantaṁ, Indriyesu susaṁvutaṁ; Bhojanamhi ca mattaññuṁ, Saddhaṁ āraddhavīriyaṁ; Taṁ ve nappasahati māro, Vāto selaṁva pabbataṁ.

_nearmiss_src.text_en: A Bhikshu (mendicant) who delights in earnestness, who looks with fear on thoughtlessness, moves about like fire, burning all his fetters, small or large.
_nearmiss_src.original: Appamādarato bhikkhu, pamāde bhayadassi vā; Saṁyojanaṁ aṇuṁ thūlaṁ, ḍahaṁ aggīva gacchati.

gold_answer: The evil-doer mourns and suffers here and hereafter seeing his evil work (1.15, 1.17), while the virtuous delights in both worlds seeing the purity of his deeds (1.16).
question: What does the Dhammapada say about how the evil-doer and the virtuous person each fare in this world and the next?

hi.gold_answer: दुष्ट अपने बुरे काम को देखकर यहाँ और इसके बाद शोक मनाता है और पीड़ित होता है (1.15,1.17), जबकि दोनों दुनियाओं में गुणी लोग उसके कर्मों की शुद्धता को देखकर प्रसन्न होते हैं (1.16)।
hi.question: धम्मपद इस बारे में क्या कहता है कि दुष्ट और गुणी व्यक्ति दोनों इस दुनिया में और बाद में कैसे काम करते हैं?

pi.gold_answer: Pāpakārī attano kiliṭṭhaṁ kammaṁ disvā idha ca pecca ca socati tappati ca (1.15, 1.17), katapuñño pana attano kammavisuddhiṁ disvā ubhayattha modati (1.16).
pi.question: Kiṁ Dhammapadaṁ vadati yathā pāpakārī ca katapuñño ca idha ca pecca ca vattanti?

_gold_src.text_en: The evil-doer mourns in this world, and he mourns in the next; he mourns in both. He mourns and suffers when he sees the evil of his own work.
_gold_src.original: Idha socati pecca socati, Pāpakārī ubhayattha socati; So socati so vihaññati, Disvā kammakiliṭṭhamattano.

_gold_src.text_en: The virtuous man delights in this world, and he delights in the next; he delights in both. He delights and rejoices, when he sees the purity of his own work.
_gold_src.original: Idha modati pecca modati, Katapuñño ubhayattha modati; So modati so pamodati, Disvā kammavisuddhimattano.

_gold_src.text_en: The evil-doer suffers in this world, and he suffers in the next; he suffers in both. He suffers when he thinks of the evil he has done; he suffers more when going on the evil path.
_gold_src.original: Idha tappati pecca tappati, Pāpakārī ubhayattha tappati; “Pāpaṁ me katan”ti tappati, Bhiyyo tappati duggatiṁ gato.

_nearmiss_src.text_en: The virtuous man is happy in this world, and he is happy in the next; he is happy in both. He is happy when he thinks of the good he has done; he is still more happy when going on the good path.
_nearmiss_src.original: Idha nandati pecca nandati, Katapuñño ubhayattha nandati; “Puññaṁ me katan”ti nandati, Bhiyyo nandati suggatiṁ gato.

gold_answer: Earnestness is the deathless path while thoughtlessness is death (2.1); the wise prize earnestness as their best jewel while fools chase vanity (2.6); the earnest advance past the heedless like a racer leaving the slow behind (2.9).
question: How does the Dhammapada praise earnestness and warn against thoughtlessness?

hi.gold_answer: ईमानदारी मृत्युहीन मार्ग है जबकि विचारहीनता मृत्यु है (2); बुद्धिमान पुरस्कार ईमानदारी अपने सबसे अच्छे रत्न के रूप में जबकि मूर्ख घमंड का पीछा करते हैं (2); एक रेसर की तरह लापरवाही से आगे बढ़ना जो धीमी गति को पीछे छोड़ देता है (2)।
hi.question: धम्मपद ईमानदारी की प्रशंसा कैसे करता है और विचारहीनता के खिलाफ चेतावनी कैसे देता है?

pi.gold_answer: Appamādo amatapadaṁ, pamādo maccuno padaṁ (2.1); medhāvī appamādaṁ seṭṭhaṁ dhanaṁ viya rakkhati, bālā pana pamādaṁ anuyuñjanti (2.6); appamatto pamattesu, sīghasso viya abalassaṁ hitvā, purato yāti (2.9).
pi.question: Kathaṁ Dhammapadaṁ appamādaṁ pasaṁsati pamādañca garahati?

_gold_src.text_en: Earnestness is the path of immortality (Nirvana), thoughtlessness the path of death. Those who are in earnest do not die, those who are thoughtless are as if dead already.
_gold_src.original: Appamādo amatapadaṁ, pamādo maccuno padaṁ; Appamattā na mīyanti, ye pamattā yathā matā.

_gold_src.text_en: Fools follow after vanity, men of evil wisdom. The wise man keeps earnestness as his best jewel.
_gold_src.original: Pamādamanuyuñjanti, bālā dummedhino janā; Appamādañca medhāvī, dhanaṁ seṭṭhaṁva rakkhati.

_gold_src.text_en: Earnest among the thoughtless, awake among the sleepers, the wise man advances like a racer, leaving behind the hack.
_gold_src.original: Appamatto pamattesu, suttesu bahujāgaro; Abalassaṁva sīghasso, hitvā yāti sumedhaso.

_nearmiss_src.text_en: By earnestness did Maghavan (Indra) rise to the lordship of the gods. People praise earnestness; thoughtlessness is always blamed.
_nearmiss_src.original: Appamādena maghavā, devānaṁ seṭṭhataṁ gato; Appamādaṁ pasaṁsanti, pamādo garahito sadā.

gold_answer: The wise straighten the unsteady thought like an arrow (3.1); taming the flighty mind brings happiness (3.3); and well-guarded thoughts, hard to perceive, bring happiness (3.4).
question: What does the Dhammapada teach about guarding and taming the restless mind?

hi.gold_answer: बुद्धिमान लोग अस्थिर विचार को तीर की तरह सीधा करते हैं (3); उड़ते हुए मन को वश में करने से खुशी मिलती है (3); और अच्छी तरह से संरक्षित विचार, जिन्हें समझना मुश्किल है, खुशी लाते हैं (3)।
hi.question: धम्मपद बेचैन मन की रक्षा करने और उसे वश में करने के बारे में क्या सिखाता है?

pi.gold_answer: Medhāvī phandanaṁ cittaṁ usukāro saraṁ viya ujuṁ karoti (3.1); lahuno cittassa damatho sukhāvaho (3.3); suguttaṁ sududdasaṁ cittaṁ sukhāvahaṁ (3.4).
pi.question: Kiṁ Dhammapadaṁ phandanaṁ cittaṁ rakkhaṇe damane ca deseti?

_gold_src.text_en: As a fletcher makes straight his arrow, a wise man makes straight his trembling and unsteady thought, which is difficult to guard, difficult to hold back.
_gold_src.original: Phandanaṁ capalaṁ cittaṁ, dūrakkhaṁ dunnivārayaṁ; Ujuṁ karoti medhāvī, usukārova tejanaṁ.

_gold_src.text_en: It is good to tame the mind, which is difficult to hold in and flighty, rushing wherever it listeth; a tamed mind brings happiness.
_gold_src.original: Dunniggahassa lahuno, yatthakāmanipātino; Cittassa damatho sādhu, cittaṁ dantaṁ sukhāvahaṁ.

_gold_src.text_en: Let the wise man guard his thoughts, for they are difficult to perceive, very artful, and they rush wherever they list: thoughts well guarded bring happiness.
_gold_src.original: Sududdasaṁ sunipuṇaṁ, yatthakāmanipātinaṁ; Cittaṁ rakkhetha medhāvī, cittaṁ guttaṁ sukhāvahaṁ.

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Those who bridle their mind which travels far, moves about alone, is without a body, and hides in the chamber (of the heart), will be free from the bonds of Mara (the tempter).
_nearmiss_src.original: Dūraṅgamaṁ ekacaraṁ, asarīraṁ guhāsayaṁ; Ye cittaṁ saṁyamissanti, mokkhanti mārabandhanā.

gold_answer: Even an evil-doer sees happiness until his deed ripens, then he meets evil (9.4); even a good man sees evil days until his good deed ripens, then he sees happiness (9.5).
question: How does the Dhammapada describe the delayed ripening of good and evil deeds?

hi.gold_answer: दुष्ट भी तब तक खुशी देखता है जब तक कि उसका कर्म पक न जाए, फिर वह बुराई का सामना करता है (9.4); एक अच्छा आदमी भी बुराई के दिन देखता है जब तक कि उसका अच्छा कर्म पक न जाए, फिर वह खुशी देखता है (9.5)।
hi.question: धम्मपद अच्छे और बुरे कर्मों के पकने में देरी का वर्णन कैसे करता है?

pi.gold_answer: Pāpopi bhadraṁ passati yāva pāpaṁ na paccati, yadā ca paccati atha pāpāni passati (9.4); bhadropi pāpaṁ passati yāva bhadraṁ na paccati, yadā ca paccati atha bhadrāni passati (9.5).
pi.question: Kathaṁ Dhammapadaṁ kalyāṇapāpakammānaṁ vilambitaṁ vipākaṁ vaṇṇeti?

_gold_src.text_en: Even an evil-doer sees happiness as long as his evil deed has not ripened; but when his evil deed has ripened, then does the evil-doer see evil.
_gold_src.original: Pāpopi passati bhadraṁ, Yāva pāpaṁ na paccati; Yadā ca paccati pāpaṁ, Atha pāpo pāpāni passati.

_gold_src.text_en: Even a good man sees evil days, as long as his good deed has not ripened; but when his good deed has ripened, then does the good man see happy days.
_gold_src.original: Bhadropi passati pāpaṁ, Yāva bhadraṁ na paccati; Yadā ca paccati bhadraṁ, Atha bhadro bhadrāni passati.

_nearmiss_src.text_en: As long as the evil deed done does not bear fruit, the fool thinks it is like honey; but when it ripens, then the fool suffers grief.
_nearmiss_src.original: Madhuṁvā maññati bālo, yāva pāpaṁ na paccati; Yadā ca paccati pāpaṁ, atha dukkhaṁ nigacchati.

gold_answer: As drops fill a water-pot, the fool fills with evil little by little (9.6) and the wise man fills with good little by little (9.7).
question: What does the Dhammapada say about how small repeated acts accumulate into great good or evil?

hi.gold_answer: जैसे ही बूंदें पानी के बर्तन में भरती हैं, मूर्ख धीरे-धीरे बुराई से भर जाता है (9.6) और बुद्धिमान व्यक्ति धीरे-धीरे अच्छे से भर जाता है (9.7)।
hi.question: धम्मपद इस बारे में क्या कहता है कि बार-बार किए जाने वाले छोटे-छोटे कार्य बड़े अच्छे या बुरे में कैसे जमा हो जाते हैं?

pi.gold_answer: Yathā udabindū udakumbhaṁ pūrenti, evaṁ bālo thokaṁ thokaṁ pāpassa pūrati (9.6), dhīro ca thokaṁ thokaṁ puññassa pūrati (9.7).
pi.question: Kiṁ Dhammapadaṁ vadati yathā punappunaṁ katā khuddakā kammā mahantaṁ puññaṁ vā pāpaṁ vā upacinanti?

_gold_src.text_en: Let no man think lightly of evil, saying in his heart, It will not come nigh unto me. Even by the falling of water-drops a water-pot is filled; the fool becomes full of evil, even if he gather it little by little.
_gold_src.original: Māvamaññetha pāpassa, na mantaṁ āgamissati; Udabindunipātena, udakumbhopi pūrati; Bālo pūrati pāpassa, thokaṁ thokampi ācinaṁ.

_gold_src.text_en: Let no man think lightly of good, saying in his heart, It will not come nigh unto me. Even by the falling of water-drops a water-pot is filled; the wise man becomes full of good, even if he gather it little by little.
_gold_src.original: Māvamaññetha puññassa, na mandaṁ āgamissati; Udabindunipātena, udakumbhopi pūrati; Dhīro pūrati puññassa, thokaṁ thokampi ācinaṁ.

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Let a man avoid evil deeds, as a merchant, if he has few companions and carries much wealth, avoids a dangerous road; as a man who loves life avoids poison.
_nearmiss_src.original: Vāṇijova bhayaṁ maggaṁ, appasattho mahaddhano; Visaṁ jīvitukāmova, pāpāni parivajjaye.

gold_answer: Conquering oneself surpasses defeating a million men (8.4), and not even a god or Mara can turn the self-conquered, self-restrained man's victory into defeat (8.5).
question: What does the Dhammapada teach about the supremacy of self-conquest over outward victories?

hi.gold_answer: स्वयं को जीतना दस लाख पुरुषों (8.4) को हराने से आगे निकल जाता है, और कोई भी देवता या मारा भी आत्म-विजयी, आत्म-नियंत्रित व्यक्ति की जीत को हार में नहीं बदल सकता है (8.5)।
hi.question: धम्मपद बाहरी विजयों पर आत्म-विजय की सर्वोच्चता के बारे में क्या सिखाता है?

pi.gold_answer: Attānaṁ jinaṁ sahassaṁ mānuse jinantaṁ atikkamati (8.4), attadantassa saññatacārino jayaṁ pana devo vā māro vā parājayaṁ kātuṁ na sakkoti (8.5).
pi.question: Kiṁ Dhammapadaṁ attajayassa bāhirajayehi seṭṭhabhāvaṁ deseti?

_gold_src.text_en: If one man conquer in battle a thousand times thousand men, and if another conquer himself, he is the greatest of conquerors.
_gold_src.original: Yo sahassaṁ sahassena, saṅgāme mānuse jine; Ekañca jeyyamattānaṁ, sa ve saṅgāmajuttamo.

_gold_src.text_en: One's own self conquered is better than all other people; not even a god, a Gandharva, not Mara with Brahman could change into defeat the victory of a man who has vanquished himself, and always lives under restraint.
_gold_src.original: Attā have jitaṁ seyyo, yā cāyaṁ itarā pajā; Attadantassa posassa, niccaṁ saññatacārino.

_nearmiss_src.text_en: If a man for a hundred years sacrifice month after month with a thousand, and if he but for one moment pay homage to a man whose soul is grounded (in true knowledge), better is that homage than sacrifice for a hundred years.
_nearmiss_src.original: Māse māse sahassena, yo yajetha sataṁ samaṁ; Ekañca bhāvitattānaṁ, muhuttamapi pūjaye; Sāyeva pūjanā seyyo, yañce vassasataṁ hutaṁ.

gold_answer: Self is the lord of self (12.4); by oneself evil is done and undone, and no one purifies another (12.9); self is the refuge of self, so one must curb oneself (25.21).
question: How does the Dhammapada present the self as one's own lord and refuge, and the limits of being purified by others?

hi.gold_answer: स्वयं स्वयं का स्वामी है (12.4); स्वयं बुराई की जाती है और उसे पूर्ववत किया जाता है, और कोई भी दूसरे को शुद्ध नहीं करता है (12.9); स्वयं स्वयं की शरण है, इसलिए व्यक्ति को स्वयं पर अंकुश लगाना चाहिए (25.21)।
hi.question: धम्मपद स्वयं को अपने स्वामी और शरण के रूप में और दूसरों द्वारा शुद्ध होने की सीमाओं के रूप में कैसे प्रस्तुत करता है?

pi.gold_answer: Attā attano nātho (12.4); attanā pāpaṁ kataṁ akatañca, na añño aññaṁ visodheti (12.9); attā attano gati, tasmā attānaṁ saṁyameyya (25.21).
pi.question: Kathaṁ Dhammapadaṁ attānaṁ attano nāthañca saraṇañca dasseti, aññena ca visodhanassa mariyādaṁ?

_gold_src.text_en: Self is the lord of self, who else could be the lord? With self well subdued, a man finds a lord such as few can find.
_gold_src.original: Attā hi attano nātho, ko hi nātho paro siyā; Attanā hi sudantena, nāthaṁ labhati dullabhaṁ.

_gold_src.text_en: By oneself the evil is done, by oneself one suffers; by oneself evil is left undone, by oneself one is purified. Purity and impurity belong to oneself, no one can purify another.
_gold_src.original: Attanā hi kataṁ pāpaṁ, attanā saṅkilissati; Attanā akataṁ pāpaṁ, attanāva visujjhati; Suddhī asuddhi paccattaṁ, nāñño aññaṁ visodhaye.

_gold_src.text_en: For self is the lord of self, self is the refuge of self; therefore curb thyself as the merchant curbs a good horse.
_gold_src.original: Attā hi attano nātho, ko hi nātho paro siyā; Attā hi attano gati, tasmā saṁyamamattānaṁ; Assaṁ bhadraṁva vāṇijo.

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Let no one forget his own duty for the sake of another's, however great; let a man, after he has discerned his own duty, be always attentive to his duty.
_nearmiss_src.original: Attadatthaṁ paratthena, bahunāpi na hāpaye; Attadatthamabhiññāya, sadatthapasuto siyā. Attavaggo dvādasamo.

gold_answer: Grief and fear arise from pleasure (16.4), from affection (16.5), and from love (16.7); one free of these knows neither grief nor fear.
question: How does the Dhammapada link grief and fear to attachment, pleasure, and love?

hi.gold_answer: दुःख और भय सुख से उत्पन्न होते हैं (16.4), स्नेह से (16.5), और प्रेम से (16.7); इनसे मुक्त व्यक्ति न तो दुःख जानता है और न ही भय।
hi.question: धम्मपद दुःख और भय को आसक्ति, सुख और प्रेम से कैसे जोड़ता है?

pi.gold_answer: Soko ca bhayañca piyato jāyati (16.4), pemato (16.5), kāmato ca (16.7); etehi vippamuttassa neva soko na bhayaṁ.
pi.question: Kathaṁ Dhammapadaṁ sokabhaye piyapemakāmehi sambandhati?

_gold_src.text_en: From pleasure comes grief, from pleasure comes fear; he who is free from pleasure knows neither grief nor fear.
_gold_src.original: Piyato jāyatī soko, piyato jāyatī bhayaṁ; Piyato vippamuttassa, natthi soko kuto bhayaṁ.

_gold_src.text_en: From affection comes grief, from affection comes fear; he who is free from affection knows neither grief nor fear.
_gold_src.original: Pemato jāyatī soko, pemato jāyatī bhayaṁ; Pemato vippamuttassa, natthi soko kuto bhayaṁ.

_gold_src.text_en: From love comes grief, from love comes fear; he who is free from love knows neither grief nor fear.
_gold_src.original: Kāmato jāyatī soko, kāmato jāyatī bhayaṁ; Kāmato vippamuttassa, natthi soko kuto bhayaṁ.

_nearmiss_src.text_en: From greed comes grief, from greed comes fear; he who is free from greed knows neither grief nor fear.
_nearmiss_src.original: Taṇhāya jāyatī soko, taṇhāya jāyatī bhayaṁ; Taṇhāya vippamuttassa, natthi soko kuto bhayaṁ.

gold_answer: One should leave anger and pride (17.1); the true charioteer holds back rising anger (17.2); and anger is to be overcome by love (17.3).
question: What does the Dhammapada teach about overcoming and restraining anger?

hi.gold_answer: क्रोध और गर्व को छोड़ना चाहिए (17.1); सच्चा सारथी बढ़ते क्रोध को रोकता है (17.2); और क्रोध पर प्रेम से काबू पाना है (17.3)।
hi.question: क्रोध पर काबू पाने और उसे नियंत्रित करने के बारे में धम्मपद क्या सिखाता है?

pi.gold_answer: Kodhaṁ mānañca jaheyya (17.1); uppatitaṁ kodhaṁ vārento sārathi vuccati (17.2); akkodhena ca kodhaṁ jine (17.3).
pi.question: Kiṁ Dhammapadaṁ kodhassa abhibhavane saṁyame ca deseti?

_gold_src.text_en: Let a man leave anger, let him forsake pride, let him overcome all bondage! No sufferings befall the man who is not attached to name and form, and who calls nothing his own.
_gold_src.original: Kodhaṁ jahe vippajaheyya mānaṁ, Saṁyojanaṁ sabbamatikkameyya; Taṁ nāmarūpasmimasajjamānaṁ, Akiñcanaṁ nānupatanti dukkhā.

_gold_src.text_en: He who holds back rising anger like a rolling chariot, him I call a real driver; other people are but holding the reins.
_gold_src.original: Yo ve uppatitaṁ kodhaṁ, rathaṁ bhantaṁva vāraye; Tamahaṁ sārathiṁ brūmi, rasmiggāho itaro jano.

_gold_src.text_en: Let a man overcome anger by love, let him overcome evil by good; let him overcome the greedy by liberality, the liar by truth!
_gold_src.original: Akkodhena jine kodhaṁ, asādhuṁ sādhunā jine; Jine kadariyaṁ dānena, saccenālikavādinaṁ.

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Speak the truth, do not yield to anger; give, if thou art asked for little; by these three steps thou wilt go near the gods.
_nearmiss_src.original: Saccaṁ bhaṇe na kujjheyya, dajjā appampi yācito; Etehi tīhi ṭhānehi, gacche devāna santike.

gold_answer: One must dig up the root of thirst lest Mara crush one again (24.4); like a tree with safe roots, pain returns unless thirst's feeders are destroyed (24.5); the creeper of passion must be cut at the root by knowledge (24.7).
question: How does the Dhammapada describe thirst (craving) as the root of suffering and the need to dig it up?

hi.gold_answer: किसी को प्यास की जड़ खोदनी चाहिए ताकि मारा किसी को फिर से कुचल न सके (24.4); सुरक्षित जड़ों वाले पेड़ की तरह, दर्द वापस आ जाता है जब तक कि प्यास के फीडर नष्ट नहीं हो जाते (24.5); जुनून की लता को ज्ञान द्वारा जड़ से काटा जाना चाहिए (24.7)।
hi.question: धम्मपद प्यास (लालसा) को पीड़ा की जड़ और उसे खोदने की आवश्यकता के रूप में कैसे वर्णित करता है?

pi.gold_answer: Taṇhāya mūlaṁ khaṇeyya mā māro punappunaṁ bhañji (24.4); yathā daḷhamūlo rukkho chinnopi rūhati, evaṁ taṇhānusaye anūhate dukkhaṁ punappunaṁ nibbattati (24.5); rāgalataṁ paññāya mūle chindeyya (24.7).
pi.question: Kathaṁ Dhammapadaṁ taṇhaṁ dukkhamūlaṁ tassa ca ukkhananakiccaṁ vaṇṇeti?

_gold_src.text_en: This salutary word I tell you, `Do ye, as many as are here assembled, dig up the root of thirst, as he who wants the sweet-scented Usira root must dig up the Birana grass, that Mara (the tempter) may not crush you again and again, as the stream crushes the reeds.'
_gold_src.original: Taṁ vo vadāmi bhaddaṁ vo, yāvantettha samāgatā; Taṇhāya mūlaṁ khaṇatha, usīratthova bīraṇaṁ; Mā vo naḷaṁva sotova, māro bhañji punappunaṁ.

_gold_src.text_en: As a tree, even though it has been cut down, is firm so long as its root is safe, and grows again, thus, unless the feeders of thirst are destroyed, the pain (of life) will return again and again.
_gold_src.original: Yathāpi mūle anupaddave daḷhe, Chinnopi rukkho punareva rūhati; Evampi taṇhānusaye anūhate, Nibbattatī dukkhamidaṁ punappunaṁ.

_gold_src.text_en: The channels run everywhere, the creeper (of passion) stands sprouting; if you see the creeper springing up, cut its root by means of knowledge.
_gold_src.original: Savanti sabbadhi sotā, latā uppajja tiṭṭhati; Tañca disvā lataṁ jātaṁ, mūlaṁ paññāya chindatha.

_nearmiss_src.text_en: The thirst of a thoughtless man grows like a creeper; he runs from life to life, like a monkey seeking fruit in the forest.
_nearmiss_src.original: Manujassa pamattacārino, Taṇhā vaḍḍhati māluvā viya; So plavatī hurā huraṁ, Phalamicchaṁva vanasmi vānaro.

gold_answer: One is a Brahmana by being rid of evil (26.6), not by matted hair, family, or birth but by truth and righteousness (26.11), and not by origin or mother but by freedom from attachment (26.14).
question: According to the Dhammapada, what truly makes one a Brahmana, as opposed to birth or outward signs?

hi.gold_answer: एक ब्राह्मण बुराई (26.6) से छुटकारा पाकर होता है, बाल, परिवार या जन्म से नहीं, बल्कि सच्चाई और धार्मिकता (26.11) से, और मूल या माँ से नहीं बल्कि आसक्ति से मुक्ति (26.14) से।
hi.question: धम्मपद के अनुसार, जन्म या बाहरी संकेतों के विपरीत कौन सी बात वास्तव में व्यक्ति को ब्राह्मण बनाती है?

pi.gold_answer: Bāhitapāpo brāhmaṇo hoti (26.6); na jaṭāhi na gottena na jaccā, saccena pana dhammena ca brāhmaṇo (26.11); na yonijā na mattisambhavā, akiñcano anādāno pana brāhmaṇo (26.14).
pi.question: Dhammapadānusārena, kiṁ jātito bāhiralakkhaṇehi vā aññaṁ, purisaṁ brāhmaṇaṁ karoti?

_gold_src.text_en: Because a man is rid of evil, therefore he is called Brahmana; because he walks quietly, therefore he is called Samana; because he has sent away his own impurities, therefore he is called Pravragita (Pabbagita, a pilgrim).
_gold_src.original: Bāhitapāpoti brāhmaṇo, Samacariyā samaṇoti vuccati; Pabbājayamattano malaṁ, Tasmā “pabbajito”ti vuccati.

_gold_src.text_en: A man does not become a Brahmana by his platted hair, by his family, or by birth; in whom there is truth and righteousness, he is blessed, he is a Brahmana.
_gold_src.original: Na jaṭāhi na gottena, na jaccā hoti brāhmaṇo; Yamhi saccañca dhammo ca, so sucī so ca brāhmaṇo.

_gold_src.text_en: I do not call a man a Brahmana because of his origin or of his mother. He is indeed arrogant, and he is wealthy: but the poor, who is free from all attachments, him I call indeed a Brahmana.
_gold_src.original: Na cāhaṁ brāhmaṇaṁ brūmi, yonijaṁ mattisambhavaṁ; Bhovādi nāma so hoti, sace hoti sakiñcano; Akiñcanaṁ anādānaṁ, tamahaṁ brūmi brāhmaṇaṁ.

_nearmiss_src.text_en: The man who wears dirty raiments, who is emaciated and covered with veins, who lives alone in the forest, and meditates, him I call indeed a Brahmana.
_nearmiss_src.original: Paṁsukūladharaṁ jantuṁ, kisaṁ dhamanisanthataṁ; Ekaṁ vanasmiṁ jhāyantaṁ, tamahaṁ brūmi brāhmaṇaṁ.

gold_answer: The body is a dressed-up, wounded, sickly lump (11.2), wasted and frail, ending in death (11.3), a stronghold of bones housing old age, death, pride, and deceit (11.5).
question: How does the Dhammapada characterize the body and its relation to impermanence and decay?

hi.gold_answer: शरीर एक कपड़े पहने, घायल, बीमार गांठ (11.2), बर्बाद और कमजोर है, जो मृत्यु (11.3) में समाप्त होता है, हड्डियों का एक गढ़ है जिसमें बुढ़ापा, मृत्यु, गर्व और छल (11.5) होता है।
hi.question: धम्मपद शरीर और अस्थायित्व और क्षय के साथ इसके संबंध को कैसे दर्शाता है?

pi.gold_answer: Kāyo cittakato bimbo, arukāyo, āturo (11.2), parijiṇṇo pabhaṅguro maraṇantiko (11.3), aṭṭhīnaṁ nagaraṁ yattha jarā maccu māno makkho ca vasanti (11.5).
pi.question: Kathaṁ Dhammapadaṁ kāyaṁ tassa ca aniccatāya khayassa ca sambandhaṁ dasseti?

_gold_src.text_en: Look at this dressed-up lump, covered with wounds, joined together, sickly, full of many thoughts, which has no strength, no hold!
_gold_src.original: Passa cittakataṁ bimbaṁ, arukāyaṁ samussitaṁ; Āturaṁ bahusaṅkappaṁ, yassa natthi dhuvaṁ ṭhiti.

_gold_src.text_en: This body is wasted, full of sickness, and frail; this heap of corruption breaks to pieces, life indeed ends in death.
_gold_src.original: Parijiṇṇamidaṁ rūpaṁ, roganīḷaṁ pabhaṅguraṁ; Bhijjati pūtisandeho, maraṇantañhi jīvitaṁ.

_gold_src.text_en: After a stronghold has been made of the bones, it is covered with flesh and blood, and there dwell in it old age and death, pride and deceit.
_gold_src.original: Aṭṭhīnaṁ nagaraṁ kataṁ, maṁsalohitalepanaṁ; Yattha jarā ca maccu ca, māno makkho ca ohito.

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Those white bones, like gourds thrown away in the autumn, what pleasure is there in looking at them?
_nearmiss_src.original: Yānimāni apatthāni, alābūneva sārade; Kāpotakāni aṭṭhīni, tāni disvāna kā rati.

gold_answer: Self is its own lord (12.4); purity and impurity belong to oneself and none can purify another (12.9); and one should not neglect one's own duty for another's (12.10).
question: What is the Dhammapada's understanding of the 'self' as lord and refuge, and the limits of one person purifying another?

hi.gold_answer: स्वयं अपना स्वामी है (12.4); शुद्धता और अशुद्धता स्वयं की है और कोई भी दूसरे को शुद्ध नहीं कर सकता (12.9); और किसी को दूसरे के लिए अपने कर्तव्य की उपेक्षा नहीं करनी चाहिए (12.10)।
hi.question: स्वामी और शरण के रूप में'स्वयं'के बारे में धम्मपद की समझ क्या है, और एक व्यक्ति द्वारा दूसरे को शुद्ध करने की सीमा क्या है?

pi.gold_answer: Attā attano nātho (12.4); suddhi asuddhi ca paccattaṁ, na añño aññaṁ visodheti (12.9); attadatthaṁ paratthena na hāpeyya (12.10).
pi.question: Kā Dhammapadassa 'attano' nāthasaraṇabhāve, aññena ca visodhanassa mariyādāya, diṭṭhi?

_gold_src.text_en: Self is the lord of self, who else could be the lord? With self well subdued, a man finds a lord such as few can find.
_gold_src.original: Attā hi attano nātho, ko hi nātho paro siyā; Attanā hi sudantena, nāthaṁ labhati dullabhaṁ.

_gold_src.text_en: By oneself the evil is done, by oneself one suffers; by oneself evil is left undone, by oneself one is purified. Purity and impurity belong to oneself, no one can purify another.
_gold_src.original: Attanā hi kataṁ pāpaṁ, attanā saṅkilissati; Attanā akataṁ pāpaṁ, attanāva visujjhati; Suddhī asuddhi paccattaṁ, nāñño aññaṁ visodhaye.

_gold_src.text_en: Let no one forget his own duty for the sake of another's, however great; let a man, after he has discerned his own duty, be always attentive to his duty.
_gold_src.original: Attadatthaṁ paratthena, bahunāpi na hāpaye; Attadatthamabhiññāya, sadatthapasuto siyā. Attavaggo dvādasamo.

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Rouse thyself by thyself, examine thyself by thyself, thus self-protected and attentive wilt thou live happily, O Bhikshu!
_nearmiss_src.original: Attanā codayattānaṁ, paṭimaṁsetha attanā; So attagutto satimā, sukhaṁ bhikkhu vihāhisi.

gold_answer: Nirvana is the highest happiness attained by the meditative and steady (2.3); knowing hunger and the body truly is Nirvana, the highest happiness (15.7); and Nirvana is named the highest happiness above all gifts (15.8).
