question: How does the Dhammapada portray Nirvana or the highest happiness?

hi.gold_answer: निर्वाण ध्यान और स्थिर द्वारा प्राप्त सर्वोच्च सुख है (2.3); भूख और शरीर को वास्तव में जानना निर्वाण है, सर्वोच्च सुख (15.7); और निर्वाण को सभी उपहारों से ऊपर सर्वोच्च सुख कहा जाता है (15.8)।
hi.question: धम्मपद निर्वाण या सर्वोच्च सुख को कैसे चित्रित करता है?

pi.gold_answer: Nibbānaṁ paramaṁ sukhaṁ jhāyino dhīrā phusanti (2.3); jighacchaṁ saṅkhāre ca yathābhūtaṁ ñatvā nibbānaṁ paramaṁ sukhaṁ (15.7); sabbalābhehi ca nibbānaṁ paramaṁ sukhaṁ vuccati (15.8).
pi.question: Kathaṁ Dhammapadaṁ nibbānaṁ paramaṁ vā sukhaṁ dasseti?

_gold_src.text_en: These wise people, meditative, steady, always possessed of strong powers, attain to Nirvana, the highest happiness.
_gold_src.original: Te jhāyino sātatikā, niccaṁ daḷhaparakkamā; Phusanti dhīrā nibbānaṁ, yogakkhemaṁ anuttaraṁ.

_gold_src.text_en: Hunger is the worst of diseases, the body the greatest of pains; if one knows this truly, that is Nirvana, the highest happiness.
_gold_src.original: Jighacchāparamā rogā, saṅkhāraparamā dukhā; Etaṁ ñatvā yathābhūtaṁ, nibbānaṁ paramaṁ sukhaṁ.

_gold_src.text_en: Health is the greatest of gifts, contentedness the best riches; trust is the best of relationships, Nirvana the highest happiness.
_gold_src.original: Ārogyaparamā lābhā, Santuṭṭhiparamaṁ dhanaṁ; Vissāsaparamā ñāti, Nibbānaṁ paramaṁ sukhaṁ.

_nearmiss_src.text_en: There is no fire like passion; there is no losing throw like hatred; there is no pain like this body; there is no happiness higher than rest.
_nearmiss_src.original: Natthi rāgasamo aggi, Natthi dosasamo kali; Natthi khandhasamā dukkhā, Natthi santiparaṁ sukhaṁ.

gold_answer: The wise are unshaken by blame and praise like a rock (6.6); everyone is blamed, whether silent or talkative (17.7); and no one is ever only blamed or only praised (17.8).
question: What does the Dhammapada suggest about whether blame and praise are reliable measures of a person?

hi.gold_answer: बुद्धिमान लोग चट्टान की तरह दोष और प्रशंसा से अडिग रहते हैं (6.6); हर किसी को दोषी ठहराया जाता है, चाहे वह मौन हो या बातूनी (17.7); और किसी को भी कभी भी केवल दोषी नहीं ठहराया जाता है या केवल प्रशंसा नहीं की जाती है (17.8)।
hi.question: धम्मपद इस बारे में क्या सुझाव देता है कि क्या दोष और प्रशंसा किसी व्यक्ति के विश्वसनीय उपाय हैं?

pi.gold_answer: Paṇḍitā selo viya nindāpasaṁsāsu na samiñjanti (6.6); sabbe nindiyanti, tuṇhī vā bahubhāṇī vā (17.7); na koci ekantaṁ nindito vā ekantaṁ pasaṁsito vā (17.8).
pi.question: Kiṁ Dhammapadaṁ dīpeti yaṁ nindā ca pasaṁsā ca purisassa saddheyyaṁ pamāṇaṁ honti vā no vā?

_gold_src.text_en: As a solid rock is not shaken by the wind, wise people falter not amidst blame and praise.
_gold_src.original: Selo yathā ekaghano, vātena na samīrati; Evaṁ nindāpasaṁsāsu, na samiñjanti paṇḍitā.

_gold_src.text_en: This is an old saying, O Atula, this is not only of to-day: `They blame him who sits silent, they blame him who speaks much, they also blame him who says little; there is no one on earth who is not blamed.'
_gold_src.original: Porāṇametaṁ atula, netaṁ ajjatanāmiva; Nindanti tuṇhimāsīnaṁ, nindanti bahubhāṇinaṁ; Mitabhāṇimpi nindanti, natthi loke anindito.

_gold_src.text_en: There never was, there never will be, nor is there now, a man who is always blamed, or a man who is always praised.
_gold_src.original: Na cāhu na ca bhavissati, na cetarahi vijjati; Ekantaṁ nindito poso, ekantaṁ vā pasaṁsito.

_nearmiss_src.text_en: But he whom those who discriminate praise continually day after day, as without blemish, wise, rich in knowledge and virtue, who would dare to blame him, like a coin made of gold from the Gambu river? Even the gods praise him, he is praised even by Brahman.
_nearmiss_src.original: Yañce viññū pasaṁsanti, anuvicca suve suve; Acchiddavuttiṁ medhāviṁ, paññāsīlasamāhitaṁ.

gold_answer: If one finds no equal or better, one should keep a solitary journey, for there is no companionship with a fool (5.2, 23.11); finding no prudent companion, walk alone like an elephant in the forest (23.10).
question: How does the Dhammapada weigh solitude against companionship on the path?

hi.gold_answer: यदि किसी को कोई समान या बेहतर नहीं मिलता है, तो उसे एकांत यात्रा करनी चाहिए, क्योंकि मूर्ख के साथ कोई साहचर्य नहीं है (5,2, 23.11); कोई विवेकपूर्ण साथी न पाकर, जंगल में हाथी की तरह अकेले चलें (23.10)।
hi.question: धम्मपद मार्ग पर साहचर्य के खिलाफ एकांत को कैसे मापता है?

pi.gold_answer: Sace seyyaṁ sadisaṁ vā na labhati, ekacariyaṁ daḷhaṁ kareyya, natthi hi bāle sahāyatā (5.2, 23.11); nipakaṁ sahāyaṁ alabhanto, mātaṅgaraññe nāgo viya eko care (23.10).
pi.question: Kathaṁ Dhammapadaṁ magge ekacariyaṁ sahāyatāya saddhiṁ tuleti?

_gold_src.text_en: If a traveller does not meet with one who is his better, or his equal, let him firmly keep to his solitary journey; there is no companionship with a fool.
_gold_src.original: Carañce nādhigaccheyya, Seyyaṁ sadisamattano; Ekacariyaṁ daḷhaṁ kayirā, Natthi bāle sahāyatā.

_gold_src.text_en: If a man find no prudent companion who walks with him, is wise, and lives soberly, let him walk alone, like a king who has left his conquered country behind,—like an elephant in the forest.
_gold_src.original: No ce labhetha nipakaṁ sahāyaṁ, Saddhiṁ caraṁ sādhuvihāridhīraṁ; Rājāva raṭṭhaṁ vijitaṁ pahāya, Eko care mātaṅgaraññeva nāgo.

_gold_src.text_en: It is better to live alone, there is no companionship with a fool; let a man walk alone, let him commit no sin, with few wishes, like an elephant in the forest.
_gold_src.original: Ekassa caritaṁ seyyo, Natthi bāle sahāyatā; Eko care na ca pāpāni kayirā, Appossukko mātaṅgaraññeva nāgo.

_nearmiss_src.text_en: If a man find a prudent companion who walks with him, is wise, and lives soberly, he may walk with him, overcoming all dangers, happy, but considerate.
_nearmiss_src.original: Sace labhetha nipakaṁ sahāyaṁ, Saddhiṁ caraṁ sādhuvihāridhīraṁ; Abhibhuyya sabbāni parissayāni, Careyya tenattamano satīmā.

gold_answer: Nakedness, matted hair, fasting, and dust cannot purify one who has not conquered desire (10.13); one is not a Brahmana by matted hair or birth but by truth (26.11); matted hair and goat-skins are useless while ravening remains within (26.12).
question: What is the Dhammapada's stance on outward ritual, matted hair, and ascetic practices as means to purity?

hi.gold_answer: नग्नता, गुच्छेदार बाल, उपवास और धूल उस व्यक्ति को शुद्ध नहीं कर सकते है जिसने इच्छा पर विजय प्राप्त नहीं की है (10.13); कोई भी व्यक्ति गुच्छेदार बाल या जन्म से ब्राह्मण नहीं है, बल्कि सच्चाई से (26.11); गुच्छेदार बाल और बकरी की खाल बेकार हैं, जबकि गुच्छे अंदर रहते हैं (26.12)।

pi.gold_answer: Naggacariyā jaṭā anāsakā rajojallañca avitiṇṇakaṅkhaṁ maccaṁ na sodhenti (10.13); na jaṭāhi na jaccā brāhmaṇo, saccena pana (26.11); jaṭā ajinasāṭi ca niratthā yāva abbhantaraṁ gahanaṁ tiṭṭhati (26.12).
pi.question: Kā Dhammapadassa suddhiupāyabhāve bāhirakiriyāsu jaṭāsu tapesu ca diṭṭhi?

_gold_src.text_en: Not nakedness, not platted hair, not dirt, not fasting, or lying on the earth, not rubbing with dust, not sitting motionless, can purify a mortal who has not overcome desires.
_gold_src.original: Na naggacariyā na jaṭā na paṅkā, Nānāsakā thaṇḍilasāyikā vā; Rajojallaṁ ukkuṭikappadhānaṁ, Sodhenti maccaṁ avitiṇṇakaṅkhaṁ.

_gold_src.text_en: A man does not become a Brahmana by his platted hair, by his family, or by birth; in whom there is truth and righteousness, he is blessed, he is a Brahmana.
_gold_src.original: Na jaṭāhi na gottena, na jaccā hoti brāhmaṇo; Yamhi saccañca dhammo ca, so sucī so ca brāhmaṇo.

_gold_src.text_en: What is the use of platted hair, O fool! what of the raiment of goat-skins? Within thee there is ravening, but the outside thou makest clean.
_gold_src.original: Kiṁ te jaṭāhi dummedha, kiṁ te ajinasāṭiyā; Abbhantaraṁ te gahanaṁ, bāhiraṁ parimajjasi.

_nearmiss_src.text_en: He who wishes to put on the yellow dress without having cleansed himself from sin, who disregards temperance and truth, is unworthy of the yellow dress.
_nearmiss_src.original: Anikkasāvo kāsāvaṁ, yo vatthaṁ paridahissati; Apeto damasaccena, na so kāsāvamarahati.

gold_answer: Thirst grows like a creeper, driving one from life to life (24.1); like a tree with safe roots, suffering returns unless thirst is destroyed (24.5); and strong thirst sweeps the misguided man away in its channels (24.6).

hi.gold_answer: प्यास एक लता की तरह बढ़ती है, जो किसी को जीवन से जीवन की ओर ले जाती है (24.1); सुरक्षित जड़ों वाले पेड़ की तरह, पीड़ा वापस आती है जब तक कि प्यास नष्ट नहीं हो जाती (24.5); और तेज प्यास गुमराह व्यक्ति को अपने चैनलों में दूर कर देती है (24.6)।
hi.question: धम्मपद निरंतर पीड़ा के कारण के रूप में प्यास या लालसा के कार्य को कैसे वर्णित करता है?

pi.gold_answer: Taṇhā māluvā viya vaḍḍhati, purisaṁ hurā huraṁ neti (24.1); yathā daḷhamūlo rukkho puna rūhati, evaṁ taṇhāya anūhatāya dukkhaṁ punappunaṁ nibbattati (24.5); balavatī taṇhā duddiṭṭhiṁ purisaṁ sotesu vahati (24.6).
pi.question: Kathaṁ Dhammapadaṁ taṇhaṁ satatadukkhassa kāraṇaṁ tassa ca kiriyaṁ vaṇṇeti?

_gold_src.text_en: The thirst of a thoughtless man grows like a creeper; he runs from life to life, like a monkey seeking fruit in the forest.
_gold_src.original: Manujassa pamattacārino, Taṇhā vaḍḍhati māluvā viya; So plavatī hurā huraṁ, Phalamicchaṁva vanasmi vānaro.

_gold_src.text_en: As a tree, even though it has been cut down, is firm so long as its root is safe, and grows again, thus, unless the feeders of thirst are destroyed, the pain (of life) will return again and again.
_gold_src.original: Yathāpi mūle anupaddave daḷhe, Chinnopi rukkho punareva rūhati; Evampi taṇhānusaye anūhate, Nibbattatī dukkhamidaṁ punappunaṁ.

_gold_src.text_en: He whose thirst running towards pleasure is exceeding strong in the thirty-six channels, the waves will carry away that misguided man, viz. his desires which are set on passion.
_gold_src.original: Yassa chattiṁsati sotā, manāpasavanā bhusā; Mahāvahanti duddiṭṭhiṁ, saṅkappā rāganissitā.

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Whomsoever this fierce thirst overcomes, full of poison, in this world, his sufferings increase like the abounding Birana grass.
_nearmiss_src.original: Yaṁ esā sahate jammī, taṇhā loke visattikā; Sokā tassa pavaḍḍhanti, abhivaṭṭhaṁva bīraṇaṁ.

gold_answer: Like a withered leaf one stands at death's door with no provision (18.1); life has ended, death is near, with no resting-place on the road (18.3); and no son, father, or kin can help one whom death has seized (20.16).
question: What does the Dhammapada teach about facing death unprepared, without provision or resting-place for the journey?

hi.gold_answer: एक सूखे पत्ते की तरह कोई भी व्यक्ति बिना किसी प्रावधान के मृत्यु के द्वार पर खड़ा होता है (18.1); जीवन समाप्त हो गया है, मृत्यु निकट है, सड़क पर कोई विश्राम स्थान नहीं है (18.3); और कोई भी बेटा, पिता या रिश्तेदार उस व्यक्ति की मदद नहीं कर सकता है जिसे मृत्यु ने जब्त कर लिया है (20.16)।
hi.question: धम्मपद बिना तैयारी के, यात्रा के लिए प्रावधान या विश्राम-स्थान के बिना मृत्यु का सामना करने के बारे में क्या सिखाता है?

pi.gold_answer: Paṇḍupalāso viya yamadvāre tiṭṭhati, pātheyyaṁ cassa na vijjati (18.1); jīvitaṁ khīṇaṁ, maccu santike, antarā vāso natthi (18.3); antakena gahitaṁ na puttā na pitā na ñātī tāyanti (20.16).
pi.question: Kiṁ Dhammapadaṁ apariyattaṁ, pātheyyena vāsena ca vinā, maraṇābhimukhaṁ deseti?

_gold_src.text_en: Thou art now like a sear leaf, the messengers of death (Yama) have come near to thee; thou standest at the door of thy departure, and thou hast no provision for thy journey.
_gold_src.original: Paṇḍupalāsova dānisi, Yamapurisāpi ca te upaṭṭhitā; Uyyogamukhe ca tiṭṭhasi, Pātheyyampi ca te na vijjati.

_gold_src.text_en: Thy life has come to an end, thou art come near to death (Yama), there is no resting-place for thee on the road, and thou hast no provision for thy journey.
_gold_src.original: Upanītavayo ca dānisi, Sampayātosi yamassa santikaṁ; Vāso te natthi antarā, Pātheyyampi ca te na vijjati.

_gold_src.text_en: Sons are no help, nor a father, nor relations; there is no help from kinsfolk for one whom death has seized.
_gold_src.original: Na santi puttā tāṇāya, na pitā nāpi bandhavā; Antakenādhipannassa, natthi ñātīsu tāṇatā.

_nearmiss_src.text_en: Make thyself an island, work hard, be wise! When thy impurities are blown away, and thou art free from guilt, thou wilt enter into the heavenly world of the elect (Ariya).
_nearmiss_src.original: So karohi dīpamattano, Khippaṁ vāyama paṇḍito bhava; Niddhantamalo anaṅgaṇo, Dibbaṁ ariyabhūmiṁ upehisi.
